Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System
I am an American considering dental tourism at Dr. Marco Muñoz Cavallini International Dental Clinic in San José, Costa Rica (aestheticdentistrycr.com) for major restorative dental work — likely implants, crowns, or full-mouth restoration (All-on-X). I have no framework for evaluating an international dental clinic, planning medical travel logistics, coordinating pre-operative records transfer, managing the financial comparison between US and Costa Rica pricing, navigating post-operative recovery in a foreign country, or maintaining continuity of care with a stateside dentist after returning home. I need a complete operational control system that takes me from initial research and clinic evaluation through travel planning, treatment execution, onsite recovery, and long-term follow-up — with decision gates, checklists, cost worksheets, and timeline templates at every stage. The system should be specific to the Cavallini clinic's infrastructure (onsite hotels, onsite lab, onsite pharmacy, IV sedation availability, Nobel Biocare/Zimmer implant systems, lifetime warranty program) and Costa Rica as the destination country.

Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System

Select, schedule, complete, and stabilize a Costa Rica restorative dental treatment plan at Cavallini clinic within 3 to 18 months from initial inquiry, with a documented treatment decision, verified price comparison versus US alternatives, complete pre-op records accepted before travel, no unmanaged post-op complication in the first 30 days after each treatment trip, and a confirmed US follow-up provider before final return home.

Time Horizon
3–18 months from initial clinic evaluation to 12-month post-final prosthetic follow-up, depending on whether treatment is single-trip crowns/veneers or staged implant/All-on-X restoration

Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 2
System Overview

Operational Dashboard

System Objective
Select, schedule, complete, and stabilize a Costa Rica restorative dental treatment plan at Cavallini clinic within 3 to 18 months from initial inquiry, with a documented treatment decision, verified price comparison versus US alternatives, complete pre-op records accepted before travel, no unmanaged post-op complication in the first 30 days after each treatment trip, and a confirmed US follow-up provider before final return home.
6
Control Domains
3–18 months from initial clinic evaluation to 12-month post-final prosthetic follow-up, depending on whether treatment is single-trip crowns/veneers or staged implant/All-on-X restoration
Time Horizon
34
Worksheets
Milestones
  1. 1
    Cavallini Clinic Screening Complete
    Patient has verified clinic identity, treatment capabilities relevant to the case, implant systems under consideration, sedation availability, onsite hotel/lab/pharmacy infrastructure, and has opened a documented due diligence file.
  2. 2
    Diagnostic Record Packet Accepted by Clinic
    Cavallini clinic confirms receipt and usability of required US records sufficient to issue a preliminary treatment recommendation or identifies exact missing items preventing planning.
  3. 3
    All-In Costa Rica Financial Case Approved
    Patient completes a written Costa Rica-versus-US cost comparison including travel and contingency reserve and records a go/no-go decision based on total expected spend rather than procedure quote alone.
  4. 4
    Clinical Go/No-Go and Treatment Scope Decision
    Patient receives a documented preliminary treatment plan specifying expected procedure class such as crowns, implants, or All-on-X and formally decides whether to proceed with Cavallini.
  5. 5
    Travel and Sedation Readiness Locked
    Passport validity, flights, lodging, airport transfer plan, payment method, medication packing, and companion support for IV sedation if required are all confirmed before departure.
  6. 6
    In-Person Costa Rica Treatment Plan Reconfirmed
    After onsite examination and any updated imaging, the final scope, consent, timing, and price impact of any case change are documented before irreversible treatment begins.
  7. 7
    Immediate Post-Procedure Recovery Stable
    Patient has filled medications, received written post-op instructions, completed early recovery monitoring, and is clinically stable in hotel recovery without unaddressed bleeding, pain escalation, or sedation-related concern.
  8. 8
    US Return with Continuity-of-Care Handoff Active
    Before or immediately upon return, the patient has a named US dentist, transmitted treatment records, and a scheduled follow-up visit if required by the restorative plan.
Success Criteria
  • Cavallini clinic due diligence is documented before any deposit is paid, including confirmation of treatment capability for the patient's procedure type and written capture of warranty terms and exclusions.
  • 100% of required pre-op records requested by Cavallini are transmitted and acknowledged as received no later than 21 days before the first treatment-related departure date, unless the clinic specifies a different deadline in writing.
  • A written US-versus-Costa Rica cost worksheet is completed before booking travel and includes procedure quote, airfare, lodging, local transport, medication, missed work, and a complication reserve rather than procedure price alone.
  • A formal go/no-go decision is recorded before any irreversible treatment step, with the expected Costa Rica total cost not exceeding the patient's approved budget ceiling by more than 10% without a new approval decision.
  • If IV sedation is used, a same-day escort plan is confirmed before the procedure date and discharge is completed without the patient self-transporting after sedation.
  • The patient departs Costa Rica only after receiving final records for that treatment stage, medication instructions, and a documented escalation path for post-op concerns.
  • A US follow-up dentist is identified and contacted before final return home, or the case is flagged as high-risk and not closed operationally.
  • No unmanaged post-operative complication requiring emergency hospitalization occurs within 30 days of each Costa Rica treatment trip.
  • At least one post-return follow-up interaction with a US dentist or Cavallini clinic occurs within 14 days of return if the procedure involved surgery, implant placement, or major full-mouth restoration.
  • All warranty-relevant issues are documented and reported within the timeline required by Cavallini's written warranty terms, preserving eligibility for review or corrective action.
System

Section 01

System Architecture

A US patient committing to implants, crowns, or All-on-X treatment at Dr. Marco Muñoz Cavallini International Dental Clinic must evaluate a

Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 6 control domains
System Architecture

Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System

Operating Premise
A US patient committing to implants, crowns, or All-on-X treatment at Dr. Marco Muñoz Cavallini International Dental Clinic must evaluate a foreign provider, transfer diagnostic records across borders, prepay or reserve high-cost care, travel internationally for an irreversible dental procedure, recover in Costa Rica, and secure US follow-up support; if any handoff fails before surgery or before return to the US, the patient can face unusable dental work, warranty gaps, unmanaged complications, or total treatment cost exceeding US care.
System Objective
Select, schedule, complete, and stabilize a Costa Rica restorative dental treatment plan at Cavallini clinic within 3 to 18 months from initial inquiry, with a documented treatment decision, verified price comparison versus US alternatives, complete pre-op records accepted before travel, no unmanaged post-op complication in the first 30 days after each treatment trip, and a confirmed US follow-up provider before final return home.
Control Domains
domain-01

Cavallini Clinic Due Diligence and Treatment Suitability Verification

Determines whether Dr. Marco Muñoz Cavallini International Dental Clinic is an acceptable provider and whether the patient's case is clinically suitable for Costa Rica-based restorative treatment before any deposit, travel booking, or irreversible tooth preparation.

In Scope
  • Clinic credential verification for dentist, specialists, sedation capability, implant brands, onsite lab, onsite pharmacy, and onsite hotel infrastructure
  • Validation of whether the patient's case type fits cross-border care: crowns, implant placement, implant-supported bridges, All-on-X, or full-mouth restoration
  • Review of clinic communication responsiveness, treatment planning process, and willingness to review US records before travel
  • Assessment of lifetime warranty terms and exclusions for prosthetics and implants
  • Decision on whether Costa Rica treatment remains under consideration versus eliminated from options
Outputs: Clinic due diligence file · Cavallini go/no-go decision record · Case suitability determination for dental tourism · Warranty terms summary specific to proposed treatment · Provider comparison sheet versus at least one US alternative
domain-02

US Diagnostic Record Collection and Cross-Border Treatment Planning Transfer

Ensures the clinic receives complete, usable pre-operative information early enough to issue a reliable treatment plan and avoid case changes after arrival in Costa Rica.

In Scope
  • Collection of panoramic X-rays, CBCT if available, intraoral photos, periodontal charting, treatment history, medication list, and medical clearance requirements
  • Secure transfer of records from US providers to Cavallini clinic
  • Translation or formatting of records if required by clinic intake process
  • Clarification of missing records before treatment date is confirmed
  • Confirmation that Cavallini has accepted records as sufficient for provisional treatment planning
Outputs: Pre-op records packet · Records transmission log · Clinic acknowledgement of records receipt · Diagnostic sufficiency confirmation · Preliminary treatment plan request dossier
domain-03

US-vs-Costa Rica Restorative Cost and Payment Decision Control

Creates a true all-in financial comparison so the patient knows whether Cavallini treatment remains financially justified after travel, recovery, contingency, and revision exposure are included.

In Scope
  • Collection of Cavallini treatment quote by procedure type and implant/prosthetic brand
  • Collection of at least one comparable US quote for the same restorative scope
  • Inclusion of airfare, local ground transport, hotel nights, companion travel if required, food, medications, missed work, revision reserve, and follow-up trip risk
  • Deposit schedule, payment timing, foreign transaction considerations, refund terms, and cancellation exposure
  • Decision on affordability threshold and financing requirements
Outputs: All-in Costa Rica treatment budget · Comparable US treatment cost worksheet · Net savings or overrun decision sheet · Deposit and payment calendar · Complication contingency reserve amount
domain-04

Costa Rica Medical Travel and Surgical Trip Readiness

Coordinates the patient's ability to arrive in San José medically, legally, and operationally ready for treatment at the clinic's required date and condition.

In Scope
  • Passport validity check and travel document readiness
  • Flight selection aligned to pre-op appointment, surgery date, sedation restrictions, and recovery duration
  • Use of Cavallini-related onsite hotel lodging or equivalent near-clinic lodging
  • Ground transport planning between airport, hotel, clinic, and pharmacy needs
  • Companion attendance planning if IV sedation or immediate post-op assistance is required
  • Packing plan for medications, records, payment instruments, and post-op recovery supplies
Outputs: Travel readiness checklist · Confirmed itinerary linked to treatment calendar · Lodging reservation record · Sedation-day escort plan · Trip contact sheet for clinic, hotel, airline, and emergency support
domain-05

Cavallini Procedure Execution and In-Country Recovery Stabilization

Controls what happens from arrival through immediate recovery so that treatment scope, consent, medications, and complication response are documented before the patient leaves Costa Rica.

In Scope
  • Arrival-day and pre-op consultation confirmation
  • Final review of treatment plan, informed consent, and any scope changes after in-person diagnostics
  • Use of onsite lab coordination for restorations where applicable
  • Use of onsite pharmacy for prescribed post-op medications
  • Monitoring of sedation-day discharge instructions and early recovery checks
  • Verification that the patient is clinically cleared for hotel recovery, local movement, and eventual air travel
Outputs: Signed final treatment consent package · Procedure completion record · Medication dispensing record · Post-op instruction set · Fit/function/aesthetic acceptance notes before departure · Travel-home clinical clearance note or equivalent discharge confirmation
domain-06

US Reentry Continuity of Care and Cavallini Warranty Preservation

Prevents the common dental tourism failure in which the patient returns home without a maintenance dentist, complication pathway, or documentation needed to preserve warranty rights.

In Scope
  • Identification and confirmation of a US dentist willing to provide hygiene, monitoring, minor adjustment, or emergency evaluation after Costa Rica treatment
  • Transfer of final records from Cavallini to the US follow-up provider
  • Scheduling of post-return exams and imaging at required intervals
  • Documentation of any complication, breakage, implant issue, bite issue, or prosthetic defect within warranty reporting windows
  • Assessment of whether a return trip to Costa Rica is required under warranty terms
Outputs: US follow-up provider confirmation · Post-return care calendar · Complete treatment record archive · Warranty compliance log · Complication escalation pathway · 12-month restorative status review
Known Failure Modes
  • ✕ The patient compares only Cavallini procedure pricing to a US quote and omits flights, hotel recovery days, medication, missed work, and revision reserve; the apparent savings disappears after commitment, creating mid-treatment funding shortfall.
  • ✕ US records are incomplete or too old, but travel is booked anyway; after arrival, the clinic changes scope or delays treatment because anatomy, infection, bone condition, or periodontal status differs from assumptions.
  • ✕ The patient authorizes implant placement, tooth preparation, or All-on-X extractions before final in-person confirmation of scope and cost; once teeth are altered or extracted, reversal is no longer possible and negotiating leverage is gone.
  • ✕ The patient undergoes IV sedation without a reliable escort or recovery support plan; discharge, medication compliance, and immediate complication monitoring fail during the highest-risk post-op period.
  • ✕ A post-operative issue such as bite instability, prosthetic fracture, swelling escalation, suspected infection, or implant concern emerges after return to the US, but no stateside dentist is willing to evaluate foreign work, delaying intervention beyond the easiest correction window.
  • ✕ The patient leaves Costa Rica without operative notes, implant system details, prosthetic specifications, or imaging; later US providers cannot service the case efficiently and warranty claims lack required documentation.
  • ✕ The patient assumes a 'lifetime warranty' guarantees no-cost correction under all conditions, but exclusions for maintenance failures, non-compliance, outside modifications, or missed reporting windows void practical coverage after the point of failure.
  • ✕ Flights are scheduled too tightly after surgery, causing the patient to travel while unstable, miss a required Cavallini review, or lose the chance to correct an immediate fit/function problem before international departure.
  • ✕ The patient relies on a single preliminary quote without documenting what triggers price changes for grafting, extractions, temporaries, sedation, or prosthetic upgrades; final invoice exceeds approved funds after arrival.
  • ✕ A US maintenance plan is never activated, leading to missed hygiene, occlusal adjustments, or early implant/prosthetic monitoring, with failures surfacing months later when corrective action is more invasive and expensive.
Operating Constraints
⊘ Treatment planning accuracy is limited by the completeness and recency of US diagnostic records before in-person evaluation in Costa Rica.
⊘ Major restorative work such as implants or All-on-X may require staged treatment over multiple Costa Rica trips rather than a single visit.
⊘ Irreversible dental decisions may occur under travel pressure because the patient is already in-country and financially committed to flights, lodging, and time off work.
⊘ Costa Rica treatment requires valid US traveler identification documents and sufficient travel flexibility to remain in-country if recovery or treatment timing changes.
⊘ If IV sedation is used, same-day self-management capacity is reduced and a discharge support plan may be operationally necessary.
⊘ The patient's final all-in cost is affected by exchange rates, card processing, wire timing, refund policies, and unplanned changes in treatment scope.
⊘ US dental insurance may provide limited or no reimbursement for out-of-country treatment, so the system must operate assuming high out-of-pocket exposure unless verified otherwise.
⊘ Continuity of care after return is constrained by whether a US dentist is willing to monitor or service work initiated by a foreign clinic.
⊘ Warranty preservation depends on compliance with Cavallini's written terms, documentation retention, maintenance behavior, and timely reporting of defects or complications.
⊘ Clinical compatibility for future maintenance is constrained by the specific restorative components and implant systems used, including Nobel Biocare or Zimmer where applicable.
⊘ Use of the clinic's onsite hotel, lab, pharmacy, and integrated infrastructure may improve convenience but does not eliminate the need for independent verification of records, costs, consent terms, and discharge readiness.
⊘ The system must account for Costa Rica as the treatment destination, including international flight timing, local transport in San José, and recovery in a foreign country away from the patient's normal medical support network.
Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 6
01
Chapter 01

Cavallini Clinic Due Diligence and Treatment Suitability Verification

Cavallini Credential Verification, Cross-Border Case Fit Screening, and Lifetime Warranty Boundary Review

Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 5 control domains
Chapter 1 | Cavallini Clinic Due Diligence and Treatment Suitability Verification

Cavallini Credential Verification, Cross-Border Case Fit Screening, and Lifetime Warranty Boundary Review

What this section is for
This chapter determines whether Dr. Marco Muñoz Cavallini International Dental Clinic is acceptable for your specific restorative case and cross-border treatment model before any money, travel, or irreversible dentistry is authorized.
When it matters
Use this chapter at initial clinic evaluation and complete it before paying a deposit, reserving a surgery date, booking airfare, or allowing any tooth preparation tied to Costa Rica treatment.
Failure looks like
You rely on website claims or broad verbal assurances, commit funds or travel, and later discover missing records review, unclear sedation logistics, limited warranty access, or a case complexity that does not fit safe cross-border execution.
This chapter produces
  • Cavallini go/HOLD/no-go decision record
  • Clinic due diligence file with verified infrastructure and capability evidence
  • Cross-border case-fit determination for your likely restorative scope
  • Written lifetime warranty boundary summary with return-trip exposure notes
  • Provider comparison sheet versus at least one U.S. restorative option
Minimum Viable Actions (Start Here)
Action 1
Build the Cavallini Clinic Infrastructure and Restorative Capability Inventory in ws-01-a using the website and any direct clinic materials.
Action 2
Verify whether Cavallini will review U.S. diagnostic records before travel and specify the minimum records required for preliminary treatment planning.
Action 3
Classify your likely case in ws-01-c as localized crowns, limited implant restoration, multi-unit implant/prosthetic case, or full-arch/All-on-X reconstruction.
Action 4
Extract the lifetime warranty terms in ws-01-d and identify exclusions, maintenance conditions, and whether corrective work requires return travel to Costa Rica.
Action 5
Track communication reliability by logging response time, answer completeness, and missed callbacks or promised documents.
Action 6
Complete ws-01-e only after critical capabilities are verified and unresolved items are either closed or explicitly marked as blocking.
Orientation Snapshot

Orientation Snapshot: This chapter is the admission control gate for the entire Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System. Its purpose is not to decide whether dental treatment is needed in general, but to determine whether Dr. Marco Muñoz Cavallini International Dental Clinic remains an acceptable provider for this specific U.S. patient, this specific restorative scope, and this specific cross-border operating model. Until this chapter is complete, no deposit, no flight booking, no surgery date hold, and no irreversible tooth preparation is authorized.

The stakes are high because crowns, implants, implant-supported bridges, and All-on-X treatment are not interchangeable consumer purchases. Once the patient commits to an international provider, the decision creates downstream dependencies in records transfer, payment timing, travel sequencing, sedation support, recovery logistics, and U.S. follow-up continuity. A weak due diligence file at this stage can produce three expensive outcomes later: arrival in Costa Rica with an unusable or incomplete treatment plan, acceptance of a warranty that actually requires costly return travel, or selection of a clinic infrastructure that does not match the patient’s procedural complexity.

The required output of this chapter is a documented Cavallini go/no-go decision record, not a general impression. That record must capture verified evidence of clinic infrastructure, specialist access, IV sedation capability, onsite lab support, onsite pharmacy access, onsite hotel integration, implant system usage where relevant, communication performance, and lifetime warranty boundaries. It must also produce a cross-border case-fit determination: whether this patient’s likely scope is suitable for treatment in Costa Rica rather than requiring U.S.-based continuity from the outset.

Decision threshold: if any core capability needed for the probable treatment scope cannot be verified in writing or by reliable documentary

…

Domain Purpose
Determines whether Dr. Marco Muñoz Cavallini International Dental Clinic is an acceptable provider and whether the patient's case is clinically suitable for Costa Rica-based restorative treatment before any deposit, travel booking, or irreversible tooth preparation.
Quick Reference Rules
→ Do not pay a deposit or hold a surgery date until Cavallini confirms a record-review pathway tied to your actual diagnostics — Rationale: record-free estimates can change materially on arrival. Trigger: any request for deposit, scheduling fee, or treatment reservation before records review. Risk: nonrefundable travel loss, treatment-scope expansion, and extra hotel nights in San José.
→ Treat website claims as unverified until they are linked to documentary evidence or direct written confirmation — Rationale: marketing language is not an operational control. Trigger: when you see claims about Nobel Biocare, Zimmer, IV sedation, onsite lab, onsite pharmacy, onsite hotel, or warranty. Risk: selecting a clinic on capabilities that are missing, limited, or inapplicable to your case type.
→ If IV sedation is relevant, confirm who provides it, what medical clearance is required, and whether an escort is mandatory before marking the sedation pathway acceptable — Rationale: sedation changes travel, discharge, and first-night recovery control requirements. Trigger: planned extractions, immediate implants, full-arch work, or any procedure expected to use IV sedation. Risk: unsafe discharge, missed medication intervals, unmanaged swelling or bleeding, and inability to comply with post-op instructions.
→ Verify implant system use at the case level, not at the marketing level — Rationale: U.S. follow-up dentists may need exact brand and component information for triage. Trigger: any implant, implant-supported bridge, or All-on-X discussion. Risk: delayed stateside intervention, inability to identify components, and avoidable prosthetic instability after return home.
→ Downgrade communication reliability if three consecutive core questions require re-asking or two promised callbacks/documents are missed — Rationale: early response behavior predicts cross-border coordination quality under time pressure. Trigger: repeated incomplete answers about records, sedation, specialist access, implant systems, or warranty exclusions. Risk: later failures in treatment planning, discharge handoff, and post-op support.
→ Keep Cavallini in HOLD status if any core capability needed for your likely scope cannot be verified in writing or by reliable documentary evidence — Rationale: unresolved capability gaps must not be bypassed by enthusiasm or price appeal. Trigger: missing proof on sedation, specialist availability, records review, implant systems, or warranty boundaries. Risk: committing to a provider that cannot safely or predictably support your case.
→ Do not treat lifetime warranty language as financial protection until return-trip obligations and voiding conditions are listed in ws-01-d — Rationale: coverage can exist on paper while remaining costly to access. Trigger: any reference to a lifetime warranty for implants or prosthetics. Risk: projected Costa Rica savings disappearing after one corrective trip or warranty denial.
→ For likely full-arch or full-mouth cases, require explicit clarification of trip count, healing intervals, provisional-to-final timing, and U.S.-side adjustment boundaries before GO status — Rationale: multi-phase reconstruction creates repeat travel and follow-up dependencies. Trigger: All-on-X, full-mouth reconstruction, or multi-unit implant/prosthetic planning. Risk: underestimating total timeline, repeat-trip exposure, and post-return adjustment friction.
Decision Guide
Provider acceptability status
If: All critical capabilities for the likely procedure scope are verified in writing or documentary evidence, including records review pathway, relevant infrastructure, and warranty boundary summary.
Then: Mark Cavallini GO in ws-01-e and release structured outputs to Domain 02 and Domain 03.
Else: Keep Cavallini in HOLD and continue evidence collection only; do not authorize deposit, travel, or date hold.
Escalate when: Any core capability remains unanswered after 5 business days from first request.
Records-review viability
If: Cavallini states it will review U.S. records before travel and specifies what diagnostics are required for preliminary planning.
Then: Mark Domain 02 as unblocked and capture the required records list and contact pathway.
Else: Mark Domain 02 blocked and treat the case-fit score as downgraded due to same-week plan-change risk.
Escalate when: The clinic requires deposit or arrival in Costa Rica before any records review.
Sedation pathway approval
If: IV sedation availability is confirmed for the planned procedure type, including provider, pre-clearance requirements, discharge rules, and escort expectations.
Then: Record sedation as verified in ws-01-a and note downstream escort and lodging constraints for Domain 04.
Else: Mark the sedation pathway unresolved and withhold GO status for any sedation-dependent procedure.
Escalate when: Sedation is advertised but the clinic cannot specify first-night recovery or discharge requirements.
Cross-border case-fit decision
If: Your likely case band, travel tolerance, escort availability, work flexibility, and U.S. follow-up prospects make multi-phase Costa Rica treatment operationally realistic.
Then: Continue with Cavallini evaluation and note case-fit conditions in ws-01-c.
Else: Mark the case conditional HOLD or NO-GO for cross-border execution even if the clinic itself appears clinically acceptable.
Escalate when: The likely treatment requires multiple trips over 3 to 18 months and you cannot sustain the trip structure.
Warranty usability determination
If: Coverage, exclusions, maintenance requirements, outside-provider modification limits, and return-to-Costa-Rica obligations are supplied in writing.
Then: Complete ws-01-d and pass warranty constraints to Domain 03 for financial modeling.
Else: Mark warranty boundary unresolved and downgrade the apparent protection value.
Escalate when: The clinic references a lifetime warranty but will not provide exclusions or logistics in writing.
Communication reliability threshold
If: Core questions are answered specifically, in one follow-up cycle when reasonable, and promised callbacks/documents are delivered.
Then: Record communication reliability as acceptable in the due diligence file.
Else: Downgrade reliability and require management-level clarification before GO.
Escalate when: Three consecutive core questions require re-asking or two promised callbacks/documents are missed.
Implant documentation sufficiency
If: The clinic confirms expected implant system use for the proposed case type and states that brand/component details will be supplied at discharge.
Then: Record implant-system verification source and discharge-document expectation in ws-01-a and ws-01-b.
Else: Leave implant documentation unresolved and treat future U.S. follow-up continuity as at risk.
Escalate when: Implant treatment is under consideration but brand-specific documentation cannot be confirmed.
Final chapter closure
If: All five worksheets are complete, critical claims are verified or blocked with reason, and ws-01-e records GO, HOLD, or NO-GO with date and rationale.
Then: Close Chapter 1 and release only the structured outputs allowed by status.
Else: Leave the chapter open and prohibit downstream execution other than evidence collection.
Escalate when: A pressured timeline or attractive quote is being used to bypass incomplete worksheets.
Trigger Blocks
  • Trigger: Cavallini requests deposit before records review. Immediate response: classify as control exception, suspend commitment, and request written records-review policy and minimum diagnostics list.
  • Trigger: Website references Nobel Biocare or Zimmer. Immediate response: capture the source, ask whether that system applies to your likely case type, and record whether discharge documentation will include brand details.
  • Trigger: Website or staff mention IV sedation. Immediate response: confirm provider, procedure eligibility, pre-clearance requirements, escort requirement, and first-night recovery expectations before keeping sedation-dependent treatment in scope.
  • Trigger: Likely case is full-arch or All-on-X. Immediate response: require explicit trip count, healing interval, provisional-to-final sequence, and return-trip exposure analysis in ws-01-d.
  • Trigger: A core due diligence question goes unanswered for 5 business days. Immediate response: escalate once in writing and set a 3-business-day response deadline.
  • Trigger: The clinic answers with marketing language but no operational specifics. Immediate response: keep Cavallini in HOLD and move to provider comparison review before treating it as primary.
  • Trigger: You cannot identify a plausible U.S. dentist for surveillance or emergency triage. Immediate response: place any implant-supported bridge or All-on-X pathway into conditional HOLD.
  • Trigger: Warranty terms are mentioned without exclusions. Immediate response: do not count the warranty as protection until ws-01-d contains written limits and return-travel obligations.
Risk Blocks
  • Risk: arrival in Costa Rica with a materially changed treatment plan due to missing record review. Mitigation: require pre-travel record-review confirmation and block deposits or airfare until that pathway is defined.
  • Risk: selecting Cavallini based on claimed infrastructure that is convenient but not actually available for your procedure. Mitigation: verify each infrastructure element in ws-01-a and ws-01-b with evidence state markers.
  • Risk: assuming sedation support exists without discharge logistics. Mitigation: verify escort requirement, next-day review expectation, and recovery lodging implications before approving sedation-dependent treatment.
  • Risk: inability of a U.S. dentist to assist after return because implant brand or component data are missing. Mitigation: verify implant-system documentation and discharge packet expectations during due diligence.
  • Risk: projected savings erased by warranty access costs. Mitigation: model at least one possible return trip to Costa Rica in ws-01-d and pass that exposure to Domain 03.
  • Risk: false case fit where the clinic may be acceptable but the patient cannot sustain travel, PTO, escort, or follow-up demands. Mitigation: use ws-01-c to test operational fit before any GO decision.
  • Risk: communication failures during urgent phases later in treatment. Mitigation: log response times, missed callbacks, and specificity of answers now and downgrade provider status if thresholds are breached.
  • Risk: ordinary U.S.-side adjustments voiding warranty protection. Mitigation: require written clarification on whether outside-provider modifications affect coverage before approving Cavallini.
Worksheet Linkage
Cavallini Clinic Infrastructure and Restorative Capability Inventory
Use when: Use first, immediately after opening the chapter and before contacting the clinic with follow-up questions.
Unblocks: Creates the baseline capability inventory needed to identify verification gaps and define what evidence must be collected next.
Cavallini Website Claim Verification and Missing-Evidence Checklist
Use when: Use after ws-01-a whenever a website or brochure claim must be converted into documentary proof or a direct written question.
Unblocks: Separates verified claims from unresolved claims so GO status cannot be granted on marketing language alone.
Dental Tourism Case-Fit Screening Form for Crowns, Implants, and All-on-X at Cavallini
Use when: Use once the likely restorative scope is known well enough to classify it into one of the four case bands.
Unblocks: Determines whether cross-border execution is realistic for this patient before records transfer and financial modeling accelerate.
Cavallini Lifetime Warranty Exclusions and Return-Trip Exposure Summary
Use when: Use as soon as warranty language appears in clinic materials or discussions, and especially before treating the warranty as cost protection.
Unblocks: Allows Domain 03 to model return-trip exposure, maintenance conditions, and warranty-access friction accurately.
Cavallini-versus-U.S. Restorative Provider Go/No-Go Decision Record
Use when: Use last, after infrastructure evidence, case fit, communication reliability, and warranty boundaries are documented.
Unblocks: Authorizes GO release to Domain 02 and Domain 03 or formally stops the project at the provider-selection gate.
Detailed Explanation

This chapter functions as admission control for the entire system. It is not a general research step and it is not a place for impressions. Its job is to test whether Cavallini is acceptable for your actual likely treatment scope under cross-border conditions. That means the chapter must convert visible claims into verified operating facts, convert patient enthusiasm into case-fit screening, and convert warranty language into explicit obligations. Until that conversion is complete, downstream work stays blocked because every later domain depends on whether this provider can reliably support the treatment pathway you may actually need.

The first operating principle is capability sufficiency. Infrastructure claims matter only when they change control design for your case. An onsite lab is highly relevant when same-stay prosthetic corrections, provisional adjustments, or remakes could affect the length and stability of your trip. Onsite pharmacy access matters because medication fulfillment after surgery is part of discharge execution, not a convenience detail. Onsite hotel integration may be optional for a simple crown workflow, but it becomes control-critical when IV sedation, extractions, immediate implants, or All-on-X increase swelling, fatigue, bleeding risk, and follow-up appointment density. Because these items do not carry equal weight across all procedures, this chapter requires you to verify the infrastructure that is material to your probable scope rather than compiling a generic amenity list.

The second principle is records-before-commitment. A clinic may appear attractive and still remain operationally unacceptable if it will not review U.S. diagnostics before travel or cannot specify what records it needs for preliminary planning. That failure matters because crowns, implants, multi-unit prosthetics, and full-arch cases can change substantially when imaging, periodontal information, medical history, or treatment history are incomplete. The chapter therefore tests whether Cavallini can shift from general sales communication to case-based planning communication. If it cannot, the risk of arriving in Costa Rica to discover a new implant count, graft requirement, extraction sequence, or extended stay becomes too high to justify commitment.

The third principle is patient-side case fit. A clinic can be clinically legitimate while still being the wrong operating model for a specific U.S. patient. The case-fit screen therefore asks whether you can actually support the travel, escort, work leave, repeat-trip structure, and U.S. follow-up demands associated with your likely case band. This distinction becomes especially important in full-mouth and All-on-X pathways, where trip count, healing intervals, provisional-to-final conversion, and bite o

…

Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 8
Chapter 1 · Quick Reference
Cavallini Clinic Due Diligence and Treatment Suitability Verification

Cavallini Credential Verification, Cross-Border Case Fit Screening, and Lifetime Warranty Boundary Review

Operating Rules
01 Do not pay a deposit or hold a surgery date until Cavallini confirms a record-review pathway tied to your actual diagnostics — Rationale: record-free estimates can change materially on arrival. Trigger: any request for deposit, scheduling fee, or treatment reservation before records review. Risk: nonrefundable travel loss, treatment-scope expansion, and extra hotel nights in San José.
02 Treat website claims as unverified until they are linked to documentary evidence or direct written confirmation — Rationale: marketing language is not an operational control. Trigger: when you see claims about Nobel Biocare, Zimmer, IV sedation, onsite lab, onsite pharmacy, onsite hotel, or warranty. Risk: selecting a clinic on capabilities that are missing, limited, or inapplicable to your case type.
03 If IV sedation is relevant, confirm who provides it, what medical clearance is required, and whether an escort is mandatory before marking the sedation pathway acceptable — Rationale: sedation changes travel, discharge, and first-night recovery control requirements. Trigger: planned extractions, immediate implants, full-arch work, or any procedure expected to use IV sedation. Risk: unsafe discharge, missed medication intervals, unmanaged swelling or bleeding, and inability to comply with post-op instructions.
04 Verify implant system use at the case level, not at the marketing level — Rationale: U.S. follow-up dentists may need exact brand and component information for triage. Trigger: any implant, implant-supported bridge, or All-on-X discussion. Risk: delayed stateside intervention, inability to identify components, and avoidable prosthetic instability after return home.
05 Downgrade communication reliability if three consecutive core questions require re-asking or two promised callbacks/documents are missed — Rationale: early response behavior predicts cross-border coordination quality under time pressure. Trigger: repeated incomplete answers about records, sedation, specialist access, implant systems, or warranty exclusions. Risk: later failures in treatment planning, discharge handoff, and post-op support.
06 Keep Cavallini in HOLD status if any core capability needed for your likely scope cannot be verified in writing or by reliable documentary evidence — Rationale: unresolved capability gaps must not be bypassed by enthusiasm or price appeal. Trigger: missing proof on sedation, specialist availability, records review, implant systems, or warranty boundaries. Risk: committing to a provider that cannot safely or predictably support your case.
07 Do not treat lifetime warranty language as financial protection until return-trip obligations and voiding conditions are listed in ws-01-d — Rationale: coverage can exist on paper while remaining costly to access. Trigger: any reference to a lifetime warranty for implants or prosthetics. Risk: projected Costa Rica savings disappearing after one corrective trip or warranty denial.
08 For likely full-arch or full-mouth cases, require explicit clarification of trip count, healing intervals, provisional-to-final timing, and U.S.-side adjustment boundaries before GO status — Rationale: multi-phase reconstruction creates repeat travel and follow-up dependencies. Trigger: All-on-X, full-mouth reconstruction, or multi-unit implant/prosthetic planning. Risk: underestimating total timeline, repeat-trip exposure, and post-return adjustment friction.
Worksheets
→ Cavallini Clinic Infrastructure and Restorative Capability Inventory
→ Cavallini Website Claim Verification and Missing-Evidence Checklist
→ Dental Tourism Case-Fit Screening Form for Crowns, Implants, and All-on-X at Cavallini
→ Cavallini Lifetime Warranty Exclusions and Return-Trip Exposure Summary
→ Cavallini-versus-U.S. Restorative Provider Go/No-Go Decision Record
Cascade Triggers

Downstream impacts if this chapter fails.

! When the Cavallini go/HOLD/no-go decision record is marked GO, trigger pre-op record collection and transfer setup in US Diagnostic Record Collection and Cross-Border Treatment Planning Transfer.
! When Cavallini provides the minimum diagnostic requirements and contact pathway for review, trigger pre-op records packet assembly in US Diagnostic Record Collection and Cross-Border Treatment Planning Transfer.
! When the provider comparison sheet and warranty boundary summary are complete, trigger all-in quote analysis in US-vs-Costa Rica Restorative Cost and Payment Decision Control.
! When sedation, escort, and lodging constraints are verified here, trigger preliminary travel-readiness constraint capture in Costa Rica Medical Travel and Surgical Trip Readiness.
! When Cavallini remains in HOLD because critical capability is unresolved, trigger evidence collection only and block Domain 02 and Domain 03 from commitment-stage execution.
! When the case is marked NO-GO for cross-border suitability, trigger archival closure of Cavallini evaluation and redirect comparison effort toward a U.S.-based provider path.
Key Contacts
Role Name / Contact
