! Common Errors to Avoid
  • Leaving Cavallini's bundled quote intact instead of separating surgery, temporaries, sedation, medications, and final prosthetic phases, which makes the U.S. comparison unusable
  • Recording 'implants included' without the specified system such as Nobel Biocare or Zimmer, which breaks like-for-like comparison and future warranty tracking
  • Failing to mark whether final prosthetics are same-trip or second-trip, which hides the largest trip-cost driver in implant and All-on-X cases
Decision Gates
Decision Gate — g-03-a-01 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Trip Architecture Financial Usability Gate
Every quoted restorative phase is assigned to 1-trip, 2-trip, or staged multi-trip architecture; no major line item required for care remains marked Unknown.
Pass → Proceed
Release this worksheet to ws-03-b, ws-03-c, and ws-03-d as the clinical pricing source of truth.
Fail → Remediate
Mark financial status REQUIRES CLARIFICATION and suspend deposit authorization until Cavallini confirms same-trip versus second-trip delivery requirements in writing.
Decision Gate — g-03-a-02 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Like-for-Like Normalization Gate
Implant brand, prosthetic type, sedation inclusion, temporaries inclusion, and final prosthetic inclusion are each explicitly stated or explicitly marked Not Stated with follow-up pending.
Pass → Proceed
Use the normalized categories to build the U.S. comparator in ws-03-c.
Fail → Remediate
Do not compare against any U.S. estimate yet; request category clarification from Cavallini and rebuild the quote capture table.
Date Completed
Reviewed By
Repeat-use worksheet · Photocopy as needed
Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 26
CAVALLINI COSTA RICA RESTORATIVE DENTAL TRAVEL CONTROL SYSTEM Chapter 3 — All-In Cavallini Price Modeling, U.S. Comparison, Depo…
Worksheet CHECKLIST → US-vs-Costa Rica Restorative Cost and Payment Decision Control

Costa Rica Treatment Budget Inclusion Checklist for Travel, Recovery, and Return-Trip Risk

Verifies that every non-clinical Costa Rica cost driver and recovery-related exposure has been included before any savings claim or booking decision is approved.

⏱ 25 minutes on first use, 10 minutes after quote updates ↗ See also: Cavallini Restorative Quote Line-Item Capture for Implants, Crowns, and All-on-X · U.S.-Versus-Cavallini Like-for-Like Restorative Cost Comparison Matrix · Deposit, Final Payment, and Foreign Transaction Timing Planner for Cavallini Care · Complication Reserve and Savings Threshold Approval Form for Dental Tourism Commitment
What a verified item looks like
✓
Verified that a second planned prosthetic trip for final zirconia delivery was included using the trip architecture from ws-03-a and actual pricing from Delta checkout and Courtyard Escazú nightly rates — pass = airfare USD 912, hotel 6 nights at USD 148 plus tax, food USD 55/day, rideshare USD 120 entered; fail = no second-trip travel lines existed, so net savings was invalidated and the model was rebuilt.
Verify that round-trip airfare for the patient has been priced using a real itinerary from the actual departure airport to San José, Costa Rica — use airline checkout screens or a travel agent quote, not a search-result teaser fare — pass = ticket price including taxes is entered in the Costa Rica model, fail = reprice using a bookable itinerary before claiming savings.
Verify that checked-bag or carry-on fees for medically necessary supplies, recovery items, or extended-stay clothing are included using the selected airline baggage policy — pass = baggage cost is entered as its own line item or documented as zero because the fare includes bags, fail = add baggage costs before approving the budget.
Verify that the baseline lodging plan near Cavallini clinic or in Cavallini-related onsite lodging is priced from an actual booking source for the expected treatment dates — pass = nightly rate, taxes, and number of baseline nights are documented, fail = replace memory-based hotel estimates with a current booking quote.
Verify that a separate contingency lodging extension has been priced for at least one recovery-driven delay scenario using the same hotel or nearby equivalent — pass = extra nights are shown as a distinct contingency line, fail = mark the lodging model incomplete and recalculate.
Verify that airport-to-hotel and hotel-to-clinic ground transportation in San José has been priced using the planned transport method — use clinic-arranged transfer details, rideshare estimates, or car service quote — pass = all known transfers are budgeted, fail = add local transport before proceeding.
Verify that daily food spending for the patient during Costa Rica recovery has been modeled using the expected number of nights and post-op diet limitations — pass = meal budget is entered with calculation method, fail = replace vague 'food buffer' entry with a per-day estimate.
Verify that onsite pharmacy or outside pharmacy medication purchases likely to be paid in Costa Rica are included using the clinic medication list or coordinator guidance — pass = medications are entered as a Costa Rica line item or documented as included, fail = request clarification and hold the all-in total as incomplete.
Verify that companion airfare is treated as mandatory whenever the treatment path includes IV sedation, extensive extractions, or immediate post-op dependency unless Cavallini explicitly states otherwise in writing — pass = companion travel is included or a written no-companion rationale is attached, fail = classify the budget as undercounted.
Verify that companion lodging and daily food costs are included for every night the patient is expected to need post-sedation or post-surgical assistance — pass = companion stay is modeled across the correct dates, fail = revise the budget to include full support duration.
Verify that missed work for the patient has been converted into cash exposure using payroll records, PTO policy, or self-employment revenue history — pass = a dollar value and calculation basis are documented, fail = do not leave lost income as a narrative note.
Verify that missed work for the companion has been converted into cash exposure whenever the companion is required for sedation-day control or first-48-hours assistance — pass = companion lost-income impact is entered or documented as zero with supporting reason, fail = add the figure before net savings review.
Verify that passport renewal or expedited passport fees are included if the current U.S. passport will not remain valid for the planned trip architecture — use the passport expiration date and current Department of State fee schedule — pass = passport cost is entered or documented as not required, fail = add renewal cost to the travel budget.
Verify that child-care or elder-care costs triggered specifically by the Costa Rica treatment trip are included using actual caregiver quotes or usual paid rates — pass = these dependency-care costs are entered or marked not applicable, fail = revise the non-clinical budget.
Verify that U.S. pre-travel costs directly caused by the international case, such as records mailing, CBCT disc duplication, or notarized payment paperwork, are captured if not already included elsewhere — pass = these case-triggered admin costs are listed, fail = update the budget before approval.
Verify that the model distinguishes a one-trip case from a two-trip or staged prosthetic case using the trip architecture from ws-03-a — pass = all trip-related cost categories are multiplied by the correct number of trips, fail = invalidate the all-in budget and rebuild it.
Verify that a second planned prosthetic trip, if required for final zirconia or final implant prosthetic delivery, includes fresh airfare, lodging, food, local transport, and time-off-work costs rather than only the chairside clinical fee — pass = second-trip architecture is fully budgeted, fail = mark total savings as invalid.
Verify that emergency or unplanned return-trip exposure has been priced at least at a baseline level using one emergency flight, three hotel nights, and local transport — pass = return-trip exposure is entered as its own risk line, fail = the reserve model cannot be approved.
Verify that recovery-driven hotel extension risk after implant placement, All-on-X surgery, or occlusal adjustment has been included separately from the planned stay — pass = extension risk is visible as contingency, fail = rewrite the lodging model.
Verify that foreign transaction fees, wire fees, and exchange spread are included when the planned payment method will trigger them — use card issuer disclosures, bank wire schedule, and quoted currency handling rules — pass = payment friction is separately budgeted when applicable, fail = ws-03-d must be completed before budget approval.
Verify that the budget does not count any Cavallini packaged hotel, transfer, imaging, or medication inclusion twice — compare ws-03-a quote lines against non-clinical entries — pass = every packaged inclusion is either priced once or marked included, fail = remove duplicate costs and recalculate.
Verify that the U.S. comparator in ws-03-c has not been padded with Costa Rica-only travel expenses or stripped of equivalent sedation and prosthetic categories — pass = ancillary travel costs sit only on the Costa Rica side, fail = rebuild the comparison model before decision review.
Verify that non-clinical Costa Rica cost categories have been totaled and tested against the quoted clinical fee from ws-03-a — pass = if they exceed 20% of clinical fee the earlier cost conclusion is formally invalidated and recalculated, fail = no approval can be issued until that recalculation occurs.
Verify that all line items marked 'estimate' are tied to a named source and date rather than guessed from memory — pass = each estimated cost has a source note such as airline screenshot date or hotel quote date, fail = replace unsupported estimates.
Verify that the all-in Costa Rica budget includes patient cash exposure before travel, during travel, and after return instead of a single lump sum only — pass = timing-sensitive items are distinguishable for cash-flow planning, fail = send the case to ws-03-d for restructuring before sign-off.
Verify that the final budget output states whether Costa Rica remains lower-cost, marginal-savings/high-friction, justified-overrun, or incomplete for commitment based on entered costs — pass = one status is explicitly assigned, fail = the worksheet is not complete enough to trigger travel readiness.
! Common Errors to Avoid
  • Counting only airfare and hotel while omitting missed work, companion travel, and emergency return-trip exposure, which produces false savings
  • Using one optimistic hotel stay number instead of separating planned nights from contingency extension nights after swelling, occlusion issues, or sedation recovery
  • Burying card fees, wire fees, and exchange spread in miscellaneous spending rather than modeling them as a separate payment-friction category on high-value treatment
Decision Gates
Decision Gate — g-03-b-01 BLOCKS COMPLETION
All-In Budget Completeness Gate
All non-clinical cost categories required by the chapter narrative are either priced, documented as included by Cavallini, or explicitly marked not applicable with justification; no major travel or recovery exposure is missing.
Pass → Proceed
Release the all-in Costa Rica budget to ws-03-c and ws-03-e for final net-cost modeling.
Fail → Remediate
Mark financial status incomplete for commitment and hold all booking activity.
Decision Gate — g-03-b-02 BLOCKS COMPLETION
20 Percent Ancillary Cost Recalculation Gate
If non-clinical Costa Rica costs exceed 20% of the quoted clinical fee, the original cost conclusion has been invalidated and recalculated using the updated full model.
Pass → Proceed
Proceed with revised savings analysis only after recalculation is documented.
Fail → Remediate
Stop approval and rebuild the cost conclusion before any deposit, airfare, or lodging commitment.
Date Completed
Reviewed By
Repeat-use worksheet · Photocopy as needed
Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 27
CAVALLINI COSTA RICA RESTORATIVE DENTAL TRAVEL CONTROL SYSTEM Chapter 3 — All-In Cavallini Price Modeling, U.S. Comparison, Depo…
Worksheet TWO COLUMN → US-vs-Costa Rica Restorative Cost and Payment Decision Control

U.S.-Versus-Cavallini Like-for-Like Restorative Cost Comparison Matrix

Produces a decision-grade side-by-side comparison of the normalized U.S. restorative quote and the Cavallini Costa Rica pathway for the same clinical scope and trip assumptions.

⏱ 50 minutes on first build, 20 minutes for quote revisions ↗ See also: Cavallini Restorative Quote Line-Item Capture for Implants, Crowns, and All-on-X · Costa Rica Treatment Budget Inclusion Checklist for Travel, Recovery, and Return-Trip Risk · Complication Reserve and Savings Threshold Approval Form for Dental Tourism Commitment
Comparable U.S. Restorative Treatment Path
Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Treatment Path
Normalized Restorative Scope Match Test
Before you start

Before completing this section, gather the written Cavallini treatment quote captured in ws-03-a, at least one written U.S. quote for the same restorative intent, and any consultation notes that explain what is or is not included. Do not use verbal estimates or marketing price ranges. You must confirm arch count, implant count, prosthetic type, sedation assumptions, and whether temporaries and finals are included on both sides. This section is complete when the U.S. and Costa Rica pathways can be stated in matched clinical language or the mismatch is explicitly documented as blocking comparison.

Restorative case type being compared on both sides *
U.S. quote scope summary exactly as written by the domestic provider *
Cavallini quote scope summary exactly as written by the San José clinic *
Like-for-like comparison status after normalization review *
Clinical Category Cost Alignment
Before you start

Before completing this section, use ws-03-a for the Cavallini side and the itemized U.S. estimate or coordinator notes for the domestic side. If a category is missing on either side, mark it missing and do not smooth it into another line item. This section depends on the scope-match test being completed first. This section is complete when each major restorative category has a visible cost position or an explicit missing-data flag on both sides.

Diagnostics, imaging, and consultation normalization note across U.S. and Cavallini quotes *
Implant surgery, extractions, and bone work normalization note across both providers *
Abutments, crowns, bridgework, or full-arch prosthetic normalization note *
Sedation, medications, and immediate follow-up inclusion comparison note *
Trip Architecture and Access Cost Comparison
Before you start

Before completing this section, gather the trip architecture from ws-03-a and the ancillary Costa Rica costs from ws-03-b. Review whether the U.S. option is local or requires regional travel, because a fair comparison still requires noting domestic travel if material. This section cannot be completed accurately until non-clinical Costa Rica costs are captured. This section is complete when trip count, travel burden, and non-clinical access costs are visible as comparison drivers rather than buried assumptions.

Expected number of treatment trips required under the U.S. provider pathway *
Expected number of Costa Rica treatment trips required under the Cavallini pathway *
Costa Rica non-clinical travel and recovery total imported from ws-03-b *
Material domestic travel or lodging cost on the U.S. side if not local *
Net Difference and Financial Classification
Before you start

Before completing this section, confirm that missing categories have been resolved or explicitly identified as blocking. Use totals from ws-03-a and ws-03-b for the Costa Rica side and the normalized U.S. quote totals for the domestic side. This section should not be used to hide mismatches; if the underlying categories are not like-for-like, classify the comparison as invalid. This section is complete when the modeled difference is stated numerically and one valid comparison status is assigned.

Normalized total cost of comparable U.S. restorative pathway *
All-in modeled total cost of Cavallini Costa Rica pathway before complication reserve *
Modeled cost difference before reserve (U.S. total minus Costa Rica total) *
Comparison classification after like-for-like review *
Specific mismatch or risk note that could invalidate this comparison if left unresolved *
! Common Errors to Avoid
  • Comparing a full Cavallini treatment bundle against a U.S. surgical-only estimate that excludes final prosthetics, sedation, or post-op visits
  • Allowing unknown trip count on the Costa Rica side while treating the U.S. side as a single completed episode, which understates international cost exposure
  • Using a U.S. quote that lacks implant brand, prosthetic material, or arch count specificity, then treating it as a valid benchmark anyway
Decision Gates
Decision Gate — g-03-c-01 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Like-for-Like Approval Gate
The U.S. comparator matches Cavallini on restorative intent, arch count, sedation assumptions, provisional/final prosthetic scope, and major categories, or any mismatch is resolved through explicit normalization.
Pass → Proceed
Use this matrix as the benchmark for net savings or justified-overrun review in ws-03-e.
Fail → Remediate
Invalidate the comparison, rebuild the U.S. comparator, and do not approve Costa Rica on cost grounds.
Decision Gate — g-03-c-02 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Unknown Trip Count Blocker
The Cavallini pathway shows a confirmed 1-trip, 2-trip, or staged multi-trip architecture.
Pass → Proceed
Proceed with all-in cost comparison.
Fail → Remediate
Assign financial status REQUIRES CLARIFICATION and suspend deposit authorization until trip count is confirmed in writing.
Date Completed
Reviewed By
Repeat-use worksheet · Photocopy as needed
Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 28
CAVALLINI COSTA RICA RESTORATIVE DENTAL TRAVEL CONTROL SYSTEM Chapter 3 — All-In Cavallini Price Modeling, U.S. Comparison, Depo…
Worksheet → US-vs-Costa Rica Restorative Cost and Payment Decision Control

Deposit, Final Payment, and Foreign Transaction Timing Planner for Cavallini Care

Maps when money leaves the account, what event makes each payment nonrecoverable, and which payment rails are safe enough to prevent treatment-week funding failure in Costa Rica.

⏱ 40 minutes on first build, 15 minutes for updates before travel ↗ See also: Cavallini Restorative Quote Line-Item Capture for Implants, Crowns, and All-on-X · Costa Rica Treatment Budget Inclusion Checklist for Travel, Recovery, and Return-Trip Risk · Complication Reserve and Savings Threshold Approval Form for Dental Tourism Commitment
Cavallini Payment Schedule and Trigger Event Mapping
s-03-d-01
Before you start

Before completing this section, gather the written Cavallini quote, coordinator emails about deposits or date holds, and any treatment-plan PDF that states when payments are due. Use the line-item structure from ws-03-a so you can tell whether surgery, temporaries, sedation, and final prosthetics are billed together or in phases. Do not enter rough percentages without the source note that created them. This section is complete when every expected payment has a due timing, amount, and event trigger that explains why the clinic expects the money at that point.

Date-hold or scheduling deposit amount required by Cavallini before treatment dates are reserved * number
3000
Enter the deposit amount as a number only, such as 3000 for USD 3,000.
Exact calendar date or milestone when the Cavallini deposit becomes due *
Due within 5 business days of written treatment-plan acceptance
A correct entry states the real due rule, such as a fixed date or milestone tied to scheduling confirmation.
Specific event that makes the Cavallini deposit nonrecoverable *
Nonrefundable once surgery date is locked and lab planning begins
Name the actual trigger event, not vague language like 'after processing'.
Final payment amount due before surgery, prosthetic seating, or discharge * number
15500
Enter the remaining amount due for the relevant treatment phase as a number only.
Exact timing of final payment relative to treatment day *
Balance due the business day before implant surgery check-in
A correct entry specifies when final payment is expected, such as before sedation day or before prosthetic delivery.
Separate payment timing for second-trip final prosthetics if not included in first-trip settlement *
Final zirconia balance due at second-trip try-in; written terms pending
If the case is one-trip, write 'N/A - one-trip case confirmed'; otherwise state the second-phase rule.
Refund, Scope-Change, and Cancellation Exposure Control
s-03-d-02
Before you start

Before completing this section, retrieve the refund policy language from the coordinator, any cancellation terms in the treatment proposal, and notes from records-based case review. This section should be filled only after ws-03-a is complete, because scope-change risk depends on what is and is not included in the original quote. Distinguish refundable date holds from clinical deposits and lab commitments. This section is complete when you can identify exactly which payments are recoverable, partially recoverable, or sunk if the scope changes after in-person exam.

Refund rule if Cavallini changes treatment scope after in-person exam in San José *
Coordinator states deposit may be applied to revised plan but airfare and hotel are patient responsibility; no written refund percentage provided yet
A correct entry states the actual scope-change rule and notes whether it is written or verbal.
Cancellation rule if the patient withdraws before travel after paying the scheduling deposit *
USD 3,000 deposit refundable minus USD 500 admin fee until custom lab work starts; after that nonrefundable
State the specific patient-cancellation rule with thresholds and fees, not just 'see policy'.
Percent of projected total Costa Rica case cost that becomes nonrefundable before records-based treatment pathway is fully accepted * number
18
Enter a percentage number such as 18, not a decimal, representing pre-acceptance nonrecoverable exposure.
High-exposure payment structure flag based on the 25 percent pre-acceptance threshold * boolean
Enter true if more than 25% becomes nonrefundable before records transfer and written preliminary treatment pathway are complete; otherwise false.
Primary and Backup International Payment Rail Verification
s-03-d-03
Before you start

Before completing this section, gather the issuing bank disclosures for the credit card, debit card, or wire account you plan to use, and call the bank to verify foreign usage, transfer limits, and fraud controls. This section must be completed before travel booking is treated as operationally safe, because payment failure can delay surgery or prosthetic delivery. Use actual bank names, card products, and limit confirmations. This section is complete when one primary and one backup payment method are verified for Costa Rica use and the access limits are documented.

Primary payment rail designated for Cavallini charges over USD 5,000 *
Chase Sapphire Reserve Visa ending 4421 approved for Costa Rica healthcare charges
Name the exact card or account intended for the main treatment payment, including issuer and identifying detail.
Primary payment rail foreign transaction fee or currency spread percentage * number
0
Enter the actual fee percentage charged by the primary payment method, such as 0 or 3.
Backup payment rail if primary card is blocked or declined during treatment week *
Bank of America outbound wire account with daily transfer limit raised to USD 25,000 after branch verification
A correct entry names a real backup rail with known access method and limit.
Date bank, card issuer, or wire desk confirmed international usage and limit settings * date
__ / __ / ________
Enter the date you obtained live confirmation from the financial institution, for example 2026-03-04.
Payment Friction and Cash-Flow Readiness Decision
s-03-d-04
Before you start

Before completing this section, total the payment-friction costs from your card, bank, or wire information and compare them against the treatment payment amount. Review ws-03-b for any duplicated fee entries so payment costs are visible only once. This section should be completed after the payment schedule and rail verification sections are done. This section is complete when payment friction is quantified, compared to the 3 percent threshold, and linked to a clear go/hold status for financial readiness.

Combined foreign transaction fee, wire fee, and exchange spread expected on Cavallini payments * number
725
Enter the total dollars expected to be lost to payment friction, such as 725.
Payment friction exceeds 3 percent of treatment payment amount * boolean
Enter true if combined fees exceed 3% of the treatment payment amount; otherwise false.
Cash-flow readiness status for releasing deposit and proceeding toward travel booking * select
On hold pending refund clarification and bank limit confirmation
Select: Ready to release deposit / On hold pending clarification / High exposure - renegotiate or defer / Do not proceed with payment commitment
Choose the status that matches actual readiness after schedule, refund terms, and payment rails are verified.
Specific next control action required before any Cavallini payment is sent *
Obtain written refund terms for scope change after in-person exam and raise backup wire limit above USD 20,000
State the exact pre-payment control step still required, not a generic reminder.
! Common Errors to Avoid
  • Recording the total treatment fee without tying each amount to the triggering event that makes it nonrefundable, such as lab start or surgery prep
  • Assuming a credit card will work internationally without confirming fraud holds, per-transaction limits, or backup payment rails at least a week before travel
  • Ignoring prosthetic-phase payment timing, which can leave the patient short of funds right before final seating or discharge
Decision Gates
Decision Gate — g-03-d-01 BLOCKS COMPLETION
3 Percent Payment Friction Gate
If combined foreign transaction fee, wire fee, and exchange spread exceed 3% of the treatment payment amount, those costs are modeled separately and the net savings calculation is re-approved.
Pass → Proceed
Proceed with updated total-cost analysis and payment-method selection.
Fail → Remediate
Hold payment approval until friction costs are separately modeled and accepted.
Decision Gate — g-03-d-02 BLOCKS COMPLETION
25 Percent Early Nonrefundability Gate
No more than 25% of the projected Costa Rica total becomes nonrefundable before records transfer is complete and a written preliminary treatment pathway tied to actual diagnostics is accepted, unless the patient explicitly flags the case as high exposure.
Pass → Proceed
Allow payment timing to move forward to final decision review.
Fail → Remediate
Flag the case as high exposure and keep commitment on hold pending clarification or renegotiation of timing.
Date Completed
Reviewed By
Repeat-use worksheet · Photocopy as needed
Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 29
CAVALLINI COSTA RICA RESTORATIVE DENTAL TRAVEL CONTROL SYSTEM Chapter 3 — All-In Cavallini Price Modeling, U.S. Comparison, Depo…
Worksheet → US-vs-Costa Rica Restorative Cost and Payment Decision Control

Complication Reserve and Savings Threshold Approval Form for Dental Tourism Commitment

Documents whether Cavallini remains financially acceptable after reserve funding and records the binding approval outcome: net savings, justified overrun, or no-go.

⏱ 30 minutes for initial decision, 10 minutes after model changes ↗ See also: Cavallini Restorative Quote Line-Item Capture for Implants, Crowns, and All-on-X · Costa Rica Treatment Budget Inclusion Checklist for Travel, Recovery, and Return-Trip Risk · U.S.-Versus-Cavallini Like-for-Like Restorative Cost Comparison Matrix · Deposit, Final Payment, and Foreign Transaction Timing Planner for Cavallini Care
Savings Threshold and Strategic Acceptance Standard
s-03-e-01
Before you start

Before completing this section, gather the final normalized comparison from ws-03-c, the all-in Costa Rica budget from ws-03-b, and the payment exposure findings from ws-03-d. This section defines what financial result must be true before Costa Rica is approved. Do not use emotional preference or marketing language here. This section is complete when a numeric threshold or explicit strategic-justification standard is written clearly enough that a future reviewer could reach the same approval outcome.

Minimum net savings in U.S. dollars required before Costa Rica is approved on cost grounds * number
5000
Enter the minimum acceptable savings threshold as a dollar figure, such as 5000.
If Costa Rica is not lower-cost, documented reason a justified overrun could still be accepted *
Acceptable only if Cavallini offers integrated onsite lab, onsite pharmacy, and materially faster full-arch scheduling than the U.S. provider
State concrete operational reasons that would justify paying more, not vague preference statements.
Current modeled cost position after all travel and payment-friction costs but before reserve * select
Costa Rica lower-cost
Select: Costa Rica lower-cost / Cost parity within operational noise / Costa Rica higher-cost / Comparison still invalid or incomplete
Choose the modeled position reflected in ws-03-c before applying the reserve.
Dedicated Complication Reserve Design
s-03-e-02
Before you start

Before completing this section, retrieve the trip architecture from ws-03-a, the travel-risk budget from ws-03-b, and any notes about complexity level such as crowns, limited implant work, multi-unit bridgework, or All-on-X. The reserve must be real money with an identified storage location and immediate access path, not an informal intention to use a credit card later. Use the chapter rule that the reserve must at least cover one emergency return flight, three hotel nights, and local transport. This section is complete when the reserve amount, complexity tier, and access method are all documented and verifiable.

Clinical complexity tier used to size the Cavallini complication reserve * select
High-complexity full-mouth / All-on-X
Select: Low-complexity crown or limited implant work / Moderate multi-unit implant or bridgework / High-complexity full-mouth / All-on-X
Select the tier that matches the actual case architecture, such as low-complexity crown work or high-complexity full-arch treatment.
Minimum emergency return-flight cost assumption used in reserve calculation * number
1200
Enter the assumed cost of one urgent return flight in dollars, such as 1200.
Three-night hotel and local-transport assumption used in reserve calculation * number
650
Enter the combined lodging and local transport amount for an emergency reassessment stay, such as 650.
Total dedicated complication reserve amount set aside before any Cavallini deposit * number
3500
Enter the actual dollars reserved and available, such as 3500.
Where the complication reserve will sit and how fast it can be accessed *
Ally High Yield Savings ending 1184 with same-day transfer to checking; debit card backup available
A correct entry names the real account or funding source and the access speed.
Reserve Adequacy and Financial Fragility Test
s-03-e-03
Before you start

Before completing this section, compare the reserve amount against the emergency travel assumptions and the modeled savings from ws-03-c. Apply the chapter thresholds exactly: reserve must cover one emergency return flight plus three hotel nights and local transport, and if modeled savings are positive but less than the reserve, the case is financially fragile. This section depends on both the reserve-design section and the comparison matrix being complete. This section is complete when reserve adequacy and fragility status are both explicitly classified.

Reserve is at least equal to one emergency return flight plus three hotel nights and local transport * boolean
Enter true only if the reserve amount fully covers the minimum emergency travel threshold defined above.
Modeled net savings after reserve is applied * number
2700
Enter the remaining savings after subtracting the dedicated reserve, such as 2700. Use a negative number if Costa Rica is now more expensive.
Financial fragility classification when savings are positive but less than the reserve amount * select
Financially fragile - explicit sign-off required
Select: Not fragile - savings exceed reserve / Financially fragile - explicit sign-off required / No savings remaining after reserve / REQUIRES CLARIFICATION
Choose the status that reflects whether one moderate disruption could erase the savings.
Final Cavallini Financial Commitment Decision Record
s-03-e-04
Before you start

Before completing this section, confirm that ws-03-a through ws-03-d are complete and that no blocker remains unresolved. This final section records the only acceptable outcomes for the chapter: approved net savings, approved justified overrun, or rejected/no-go. If the comparison is invalid, reserve is unfunded, or payment timing remains high exposure, this form cannot be approved. This section is complete when one final status is selected, dated, and supported by a written rationale tied to the numbers.

Final Chapter 3 financial decision status for Cavallini treatment commitment * select
Approved net savings
Select: Approved net savings / Approved justified overrun / Rejected on cost control grounds / Financially underprepared - defer commitment / Incomplete for commitment
Choose exactly one final outcome based on the completed model and reserve status.
Written rationale for the final financial decision using actual modeled numbers and operational facts *
Costa Rica remains USD 6,200 lower than the normalized U.S. comparator before reserve and USD 2,700 lower after a USD 3,500 reserve; two-trip architecture confirmed; payment friction modeled; proceed with caution acknowledgment
A correct rationale cites the actual totals, reserve, trip count, and any strategic reason for approval or rejection.
Date the patient signs off on this Chapter 3 financial commitment outcome * date
__ / __ / ________
Enter the calendar date the final financial status was accepted, such as 2026-03-08.
Travel-readiness trigger released for Chapter 4 booking execution * boolean
Enter true only when final status is approved net savings or approved justified overrun and all blockers are cleared.
! Common Errors to Avoid
  • Treating Cavallini's lifetime warranty as if it pays for emergency flights, extra hotel nights, U.S. triage, or lost work, which leaves the reserve unfunded
  • Approving Costa Rica because the sticker price is lower even though the patient cannot actually set aside a cash-accessible reserve before deposit
  • Writing a vague preference such as 'I like the clinic better' instead of a documented operational reason when approving a justified overrun
Decision Gates
Decision Gate — g-03-e-01 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Reserve Adequacy Gate
The dedicated complication reserve is at least equal to one emergency return flight plus three hotel nights and local transport, and the reserve location plus access method are documented.
Pass → Proceed
Allow final financial decision review to proceed.
Fail → Remediate
Mark reserve status inadequate and do not approve final commitment even if Costa Rica appears cheaper.
Decision Gate — g-03-e-02 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Approval Outcome Gate
Either Costa Rica shows net savings after reserve and all modeled costs, or a written strategic justification for justified overrun is explicitly accepted; otherwise the outcome is no-go.
Pass → Proceed
If approved, release Chapter 4 travel-readiness trigger with the documented decision package.
Fail → Remediate
Record rejected on cost control grounds or financially underprepared and block booking execution.
Date Completed
Reviewed By
Repeat-use worksheet · Photocopy as needed
Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 30
04
Chapter 04

Costa Rica Medical Travel and Surgical Trip Readiness

Passport-to-Chair Travel Execution, Sedation-Compatible Scheduling, and San José Recovery Logistics Setup

Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 5 control domains
Chapter 4 | Costa Rica Medical Travel and Surgical Trip Readiness

Passport-to-Chair Travel Execution, Sedation-Compatible Scheduling, and San José Recovery Logistics Setup

What this section is for
This chapter turns an approved Cavallini treatment plan into a controlled Costa Rica travel operation so the patient can arrive legally, on time, payment-ready, escort-ready if required, and locally stable enough to complete treatment and recovery without schedule compression.
When it matters
It matters after Cavallini has provided a date-linked appointment sequence and before any nonrefundable airfare, lodging, or sedation-day travel commitments are locked.
Failure looks like
The patient books around airfare instead of the clinic sequence, arrives with weak passport or payment readiness, lacks a named sedation escort, underbooks recovery nights, or has no defined discharge transport, causing missed pre-op review, delayed surgery, unsafe post-op travel, or extra lodging and rebooking costs.
This chapter produces
  • ws-04-a Costa Rica Surgical Trip Logistics Inventory for Flights, Lodging, Transfers, and Escort Coverage
  • ws-04-b Passport Validity, Entry Document, and Sedation-Day Escort Compliance Checklist for Costa Rica Dental Travel
  • ws-04-c Cavallini Appointment-to-Flight Timing Planner
  • ws-04-d San José Post-Procedure Lodging and Ground Transport Routing Form
  • ws-04-e Major Restorative Dental Travel Packing and Payment Instrument Preparation Sheet
Minimum Viable Actions (Start Here)
Action 1
Stop booking activity unless the clinical sequence is documented; if missing, set status to HOLD FOR CLINICAL SEQUENCE.
Action 2
Complete ws-04-b to verify passport, entry readiness, and sedation-day escort compliance before treating tickets as final.
Action 3
Build ws-04-c by mapping pre-op, surgery, lab dependency, medication pickup, and discharge timing against actual flight windows.
Action 4
Choose lodging near the clinic that covers the expected treatment and recovery window rather than the minimum hoped-for stay.
Action 5
Assign every local movement in ws-04-d, including airport arrival, clinic attendance, pharmacy access, post-sedation hotel return, and backup transport.
Action 6
Pack and payment-check using ws-04-e so the first 24 hours remain executable even if checked baggage is delayed.
Orientation Snapshot

Orientation Snapshot: This chapter converts an approved Costa Rica treatment decision into a controlled travel operation. The objective is not to buy the cheapest ticket or fill a suitcase; it is to arrive in San José on the clinic’s usable timeline, with legal entry readiness, payment readiness, escort coverage if required, and enough local recovery infrastructure to complete treatment without compressing post-op stability. In this domain, a scheduling error is not a minor inconvenience. It can force treatment delay, increase hotel nights, break sedation eligibility, or push a patient onto a return flight before the clinic has confirmed safe travel.

The controlling principle is sequence integrity. Flights, lodging, and ground transport are downstream of the confirmed clinical sequence, not the other way around. If the appointment structure is still unstable, travel booking is premature. The common failure pattern is simple: a patient sees a low airfare, books around it, and then discovers that pre-op review, surgery timing, onsite lab steps, or IV sedation discharge requirements do not fit the flight windows.

The minimum required outputs of this chapter are five completed operational artifacts: ws-04-a Costa Rica Surgical Trip Logistics Inventory for Flights, Lodging, Transfers, and Escort Coverage; ws-04-b Passport Validity, Entry Document, and Sedation-Day Escort Compliance Checklist for Costa Rica Dental Travel; ws-04-c Cavallini Appointment-to-Flight Timing Planner; ws-04-d San José Post-Procedure Lodging and Ground Transport Routing Form; and ws-04-e Major Restorative Dental Travel Packing and Payment Instrument Preparation Sheet. If any one of these remains incomplete, the trip is not operationally ready.

Decision threshold: if Cavallini has not provided a date-linked appointment sequence with at least a pre-op attendance window, procedure date, and expected local recovery duration, then do not lock nonrefundable airfare or lodging. Output if blocked: ma

…

Domain Purpose
Coordinates the patient's ability to arrive in San José medically, legally, and operationally ready for treatment at the clinic's required date and condition.
Quick Reference Rules
→ Do not lock nonrefundable airfare or lodging until Cavallini provides a date-linked appointment sequence — Rationale: flights and hotel nights are downstream of the clinical sequence, not the driver of it. Trigger: before airfare purchase, hotel booking, or surgery-date travel hold. Risk: missed pre-op, rescheduled surgery, extra hotel nights, and broken lab or specialist synchronization.
→ Treat passport readiness as a hard gate, not a travel admin task — Rationale: expired, damaged, or timing-inadequate documents stop the trip before treatment even begins. Trigger: as soon as intended travel falls within 45 days. Risk: lost fare, disrupted clinic schedule, and forced same-week document remediation.
→ Reject arrival itineraries that do not contain a full disruption buffer before the first clinic event — Rationale: immigration, baggage delay, traffic, and fatigue commonly erase apparent margin. Trigger: when evaluating inbound flights for pre-op or surgery-adjacent arrival. Risk: missed appointments, delayed treatment start, and avoidable lodging extension.
→ Treat same-day international arrival followed by major restorative chair time as high-risk unless Cavallini explicitly structured it — Rationale: implant surgery, extractions, and All-on-X cases are vulnerable to travel fatigue and operational delay. Trigger: when arrival and first clinic event fall on the same date. Risk: unstable pre-op attendance, rushed intake, and procedural delay.
→ For IV sedation cases, a named escort is a gating dependency, not a preference — Rationale: post-sedation discharge may require a physically present, reachable companion for the first transport and early recovery period. Trigger: when the plan includes IV sedation or extensive same-day prosthetic work. Risk: sedation cancellation, discharge refusal, extra lodging cost, or postponed procedure.
→ Book lodging for the longest likely local recovery window contemplated by the treatment phase, not the shortest hoped-for stay — Rationale: swelling, medication timing, provisional adjustment, and follow-up checks can extend local stay after surgery. Trigger: when scheduling hotel nights for implant, extraction, or full-arch phases. Risk: airfare change fees, forced airport transit during active recovery, and missed clinical review.
→ Travel with one primary and one backup payment path capable of covering trip-phase charges — Rationale: foreign fraud controls or card failures can block treatment on arrival or procedure day. Trigger: before departure and again during packing. Risk: payment disputes, delayed surgery slot, and same-day operational failure.
→ Place all mission-critical documents, medications, payment instruments, and first-night recovery essentials in carry-on — Rationale: checked-bag delay cannot be allowed to break intake, medication reconciliation, or first-night stability. Trigger: during ws-04-e packing review. Risk: intake delay, inability to pay, missed medications, and destabilized first 24 hours in San José.
Decision Guide
Can travel booking proceed?
If: Cavallini has provided a date-linked appointment sequence with at least pre-op attendance window, procedure date, and expected local recovery duration.
Then: Proceed to ws-04-c flight mapping and ws-04-a trip logistics assembly before purchasing travel.
Else: Mark travel status as HOLD FOR CLINICAL SEQUENCE and stop nonrefundable booking activity.
Escalate when: Any deposit, flight, or hotel decision is being requested without a stable clinical sequence.
Is passport and entry readiness acceptable?
If: Passport is present, unexpired, undamaged, name-matched to booking, and supports departure, Costa Rica entry, and U.S. return on planned dates.
Then: Complete ws-04-b and continue to itinerary selection.
Else: Stop trip execution and begin document resolution before further booking.
Escalate when: Validity or entry readiness is uncertain within 45 days of intended departure.
Is the arrival itinerary robust enough?
If: The inbound flight provides at least one full disruption buffer before the first required clinic event and a delay of four hours or less would not cause loss of the first appointment.
Then: Mark the itinerary robust in ws-04-c and move to lodging selection.
Else: Rebook to an earlier arrival or add a pre-treatment hotel night.
Escalate when: Same-day arrival is being used for major restorative treatment without explicit clinic structuring.
Is the sedation-day plan compliant?
If: The treatment includes IV sedation and a named escort with contact information, physical availability, and lodging alignment is documented.
Then: Mark SEDATION-COMPATIBLE ITINERARY CONFIRMED in ws-04-b and ws-04-a.
Else: Mark SEDATION ITINERARY NONCOMPLIANT and ask the clinic whether the procedure can be restructured or postponed.
Escalate when: No qualified escort is confirmed by 24 hours before scheduled sedation.
Does lodging support recovery?
If: Lodging is near-clinic or functionally equivalent, covers the expected treatment and recovery window, and supports elevator access, soft-diet access, communication access, and predictable transfers.
Then: Finalize lodging and route assignments in ws-04-a and ws-04-d.
Else: Extend the stay, move closer to the clinic, or replace the property before departure.
Escalate when: The scheduled stay ends before the longest likely local recovery period for the current treatment phase.
Is payment readiness operationally sufficient?
If: At least one primary and one backup payment method can cover expected trip-phase charges and issuer/contact support is documented.
Then: Record payment readiness in ws-04-e and proceed to packing completion.
Else: Delay departure until backup payment capacity is established.
Escalate when: The backup method cannot independently cover near-term charges due on arrival or procedure day.
Trigger Blocks
  • Trigger: Cavallini has not provided a date-linked clinical sequence. Immediate response: set status to HOLD FOR CLINICAL SEQUENCE and stop nonrefundable travel booking.
  • Trigger: Passport is expired, missing, damaged, or timing-inadequate. Immediate response: stop trip execution and move to expedited document resolution.
  • Trigger: Scheduled arrival leaves less than one full disruption buffer before the first clinic event. Immediate response: reject the itinerary and rebook earlier or add a pre-treatment hotel night.
  • Trigger: Treatment includes IV sedation. Immediate response: complete ws-04-b escort compliance review before finalizing travel.
  • Trigger: Escort cancels within 48 hours of departure. Immediate response: attempt named substitute confirmation and notify the clinic before boarding if unresolved.
  • Trigger: Lodging ends before likely recovery or review needs conclude. Immediate response: extend the stay or build a flexible extension plan before departure.
  • Trigger: A payment method may not clear a large foreign medical charge. Immediate response: verify operability, notify issuer if applicable, and confirm backup capacity before travel.
  • Trigger: Any disruption changes arrival date, escort status, lodging location, or discharge transport within 48 hours of pre-op or surgery. Immediate response: same-day notify the clinic and revise ws-04-a through ws-04-d.
Risk Blocks
  • Risk: Booking low-cost airfare before confirming clinical sequencing. Mitigation: treat flight purchase as downstream of ws-04-c appointment mapping.
  • Risk: Missing pre-op review due to immigration, baggage delay, or San José traffic. Mitigation: require a robust arrival buffer and reject tight same-day arrivals.
  • Risk: Sedation-day discharge failure because no escort is physically present. Mitigation: require a named escort with contact details, lodging alignment, and availability confirmation.
  • Risk: Forced airport transit during swelling, medication use, or provisional instability. Mitigation: book recovery lodging for the longest likely local window, not the minimum estimate.
  • Risk: Procedure-day payment failure from fraud controls or single-card dependency. Mitigation: carry two usable payment methods and document issuer support access.
  • Risk: Checked-bag delay removes access to medications, records, or cards. Mitigation: place all mission-critical items and first-night recovery essentials in carry-on.
  • Risk: Undefined post-op transport leaves a groggy or swollen patient improvising after discharge. Mitigation: assign every route segment and a backup transport method in ws-04-d.
  • Risk: Clinic relies on stale travel assumptions after an itinerary disruption. Mitigation: update worksheets immediately and treat the clinic notification as an operational handoff, not courtesy chatter.
Worksheet Linkage
Costa Rica Surgical Trip Logistics Inventory for Flights, Lodging, Transfers, and Escort Coverage
Use when: Use as soon as the clinic sequence exists and before any flight or hotel is finalized.
Unblocks: Creates a single operational view needed to approve trip readiness and coordinate downstream treatment execution.
Passport Validity, Entry Document, and Sedation-Day Escort Compliance Checklist for Costa Rica Dental Travel
Use when: Use first, before treating tickets as final and again if IV sedation is in scope.
Unblocks: Confirms legal travel readiness and escort compliance so the itinerary can be considered executable.
Cavallini Appointment-to-Flight Timing Planner
Use when: Use when inbound and outbound flights are being evaluated against pre-op, surgery, lab, pharmacy, and discharge timing.
Unblocks: Approves or rejects itinerary robustness before appointment-sequenced treatment execution is triggered.
San José Post-Procedure Lodging and Ground Transport Routing Form
Use when: Use after flights and lodging candidates are known and before discharge transport is left undefined.
Unblocks: Creates a usable local operations plan for clinic attendance, hotel return, pharmacy access, and recovery monitoring setup.
Major Restorative Dental Travel Packing and Payment Instrument Preparation Sheet
Use when: Use during final pre-departure preparation once payment timing and travel dates are set.
Unblocks: Ensures the patient can attend treatment, pay charges, and manage the first 24 hours even with lost checked baggage.
Detailed Explanation

This chapter exists to preserve sequence integrity between an approved treatment plan and actual arrival in San José. Its core function is not travel convenience; it is operational compatibility with Cavallini's appointment structure, onsite lab dependencies, sedation model, and local recovery requirements. That is why the first gate is not airfare price. The first gate is whether the clinic sequence is stable enough to support booking decisions.

The chapter should be read as a dependency-control layer. Flights, lodging, and transport are not independent consumer choices here. They are support systems for pre-op review, surgery timing, pharmacy access, discharge movement, and post-op observation. A cheap ticket that compresses those events is not a savings event; it is a treatment-risk event. The same is true of a short hotel stay that assumes the fastest possible recovery instead of the longest likely one for implants, extractions, or All-on-X work.

Passport and entry readiness come first because unresolved document issues invalidate every later booking decision. The required standard is simple: the patient must be able to leave the United States, enter Costa Rica, and board the return itinerary without last-minute remediation. If that cannot be established cleanly, trip execution stops. The manual treats early clinic rescheduling as a smaller loss than gambling on document turnaround inside 45 days.

The flight planner is the most important timing tool in the chapter because it forces the patient to test real transportation conditions against real clinical events. The worksheet should not only list departure and arrival times. It should distinguish chair-time events from clinically consequential events such as pre-op review, sedation preparation, surgery, provisional delivery, bite checks, medication pickup, and discharge clearance. If the itinerary can be broken by a modest delay, it is operationally weak even if the airline considers it legal.

Lodging is part of the recovery environment, not a generic reservation. Near-clinic lodging is preferred because it reduces transfer friction, shortens post-discharge movement, and supports follow-up attendance if swelling, provisional adjustment, or medication issues arise. The right property is the one that supports elevator access if needed, communication reliability, soft-food access, predictable routing, and extension options. For this system, the hotel is part of treatment continuity.

Escort planning is separated because sedation creates a different discharge model. If IV sedation or major same-day restorative work is involved, the patient cannot rely on an informal or aspirational plan. A named person, with contact details and physical availability, must exist before the itinerary is treated as c

…

Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 32
Chapter 4 · Quick Reference
Costa Rica Medical Travel and Surgical Trip Readiness

Passport-to-Chair Travel Execution, Sedation-Compatible Scheduling, and San José Recovery Logistics Setup

Operating Rules
01 Do not lock nonrefundable airfare or lodging until Cavallini provides a date-linked appointment sequence — Rationale: flights and hotel nights are downstream of the clinical sequence, not the driver of it. Trigger: before airfare purchase, hotel booking, or surgery-date travel hold. Risk: missed pre-op, rescheduled surgery, extra hotel nights, and broken lab or specialist synchronization.
02 Treat passport readiness as a hard gate, not a travel admin task — Rationale: expired, damaged, or timing-inadequate documents stop the trip before treatment even begins. Trigger: as soon as intended travel falls within 45 days. Risk: lost fare, disrupted clinic schedule, and forced same-week document remediation.
03 Reject arrival itineraries that do not contain a full disruption buffer before the first clinic event — Rationale: immigration, baggage delay, traffic, and fatigue commonly erase apparent margin. Trigger: when evaluating inbound flights for pre-op or surgery-adjacent arrival. Risk: missed appointments, delayed treatment start, and avoidable lodging extension.
04 Treat same-day international arrival followed by major restorative chair time as high-risk unless Cavallini explicitly structured it — Rationale: implant surgery, extractions, and All-on-X cases are vulnerable to travel fatigue and operational delay. Trigger: when arrival and first clinic event fall on the same date. Risk: unstable pre-op attendance, rushed intake, and procedural delay.
05 For IV sedation cases, a named escort is a gating dependency, not a preference — Rationale: post-sedation discharge may require a physically present, reachable companion for the first transport and early recovery period. Trigger: when the plan includes IV sedation or extensive same-day prosthetic work. Risk: sedation cancellation, discharge refusal, extra lodging cost, or postponed procedure.
06 Book lodging for the longest likely local recovery window contemplated by the treatment phase, not the shortest hoped-for stay — Rationale: swelling, medication timing, provisional adjustment, and follow-up checks can extend local stay after surgery. Trigger: when scheduling hotel nights for implant, extraction, or full-arch phases. Risk: airfare change fees, forced airport transit during active recovery, and missed clinical review.
07 Travel with one primary and one backup payment path capable of covering trip-phase charges — Rationale: foreign fraud controls or card failures can block treatment on arrival or procedure day. Trigger: before departure and again during packing. Risk: payment disputes, delayed surgery slot, and same-day operational failure.
08 Place all mission-critical documents, medications, payment instruments, and first-night recovery essentials in carry-on — Rationale: checked-bag delay cannot be allowed to break intake, medication reconciliation, or first-night stability. Trigger: during ws-04-e packing review. Risk: intake delay, inability to pay, missed medications, and destabilized first 24 hours in San José.
Worksheets

Cascade Triggers

Downstream impacts if this chapter fails.

! When ws-04-c shows appointment-to-flight compatibility and ws-04-b shows travel and escort compliance complete, trigger appointment-sequenced treatment execution in Onsite Treatment Execution, Sedation Day Control, and Clinical Handoff Management.
! When confirmed arrival date, lodging location, escort status if applicable, payment readiness, and local routing are documented, trigger patient intake and procedure-day continuity setup in Onsite Treatment Execution, Sedation Day Control, and Clinical Handoff Management.
! When companion coverage and recovery supplies are confirmed for a sedation or surgery case, trigger discharge-day monitoring preparation in Costa Rica Recovery Monitoring and Return-to-Travel Clearance.
! When a disruption alters arrival date, lodging location, escort coverage, or transport within 48 hours of pre-op, surgery, or discharge, trigger same-day logistics update and exception handling in Onsite Treatment Execution, Sedation Day Control, and Clinical Handoff Management.
! When the patient has usable lodging, post-discharge transport, medication access, communication tools, and a flexible extension path, trigger recovery-environment preparation in Costa Rica Recovery Monitoring and Return-to-Travel Clearance.

Worksheet TABLE → Costa Rica Medical Travel and Surgical Trip Readiness

Costa Rica Surgical Trip Logistics Inventory for Flights, Lodging, Transfers, and Escort Coverage

Creates a single dependency-controlled inventory of every trip component required to get a U.S. restorative dental patient from home departure to Cavallini treatment attendance and immediate recovery in San José.

⏱ 35 minutes on first build, 10 minutes for each itinerary revision ↗ See also: Cavallini Appointment-to-Flight Timing Planner · San José Post-Procedure Lodging and Ground Transport Routing Form · Major Restorative Dental Travel Packing and Payment Instrument Preparation Sheet
Example entry
# Trip Dependency Component for Cavallini Treatment Sequence Booked Vendor / Responsible Party and Direct Contact Method Date-Time Window This Component Must Support Linked Cavallini Event or Recovery Function Primary Confirmation Number or Evidence Source Backup Plan if This Component Fails Current Status (Not Started / Held Pending Clinical Sequence / Confirmed / Needs Revision)
1 U.S. outbound flight to San José before Cavallini pre-op review American Airlines AA2245 + AA941 — reservations 800-433-7300 Arrive SJO no later than 1 day before 06/17/2026 9:00 AM pre-op Pre-op review for full-arch implant case with possible IV sedation next day AA Record Locator H7K2LM Rebook to earlier same-day SAN–DFW–SJO option and notify Cavallini scheduling before boarding Confirmed
2 Near-clinic recovery lodging for surgery day through post-op review window Cavallini-affiliated onsite hotel coordinator — WhatsApp +506 8888-2147 Check-in 06/16/2026; flexible through 06/21/2026 if swelling delays return Bed rest after implant placement and easy access for bite/provisional checks Hotel confirmation CVL-HTL-6184 Move to Park Inn San José with taxi route pre-mapped to clinic Confirmed
# Trip Dependency Component for Cavallini Treatment Sequence Booked Vendor / Responsible Party and Direct Contact Method Date-Time Window This Component Must Support Linked Cavallini Event or Recovery Function Primary Confirmation Number or Evidence Source Backup Plan if This Component Fails Current Status (Not Started / Held Pending Clinical Sequence / Confirmed / Needs Revision)

! Common Errors to Avoid
  • Logging only booked items and leaving unbooked but required trip components invisible, especially sedation-day discharge transport and backup lodging extension nights.
  • Using generic airline or hotel confirmation emails without recording the exact Cavallini-linked clinic date each booking is supporting, which makes rebooking decisions impossible during disruption.
  • Recording 'companion coming' without a named escort, direct mobile number, and procedure-day physical availability window for IV sedation or major implant surgery.
Decision Gates
Decision Gate — g-01 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Clinical Sequence Before Booking Gate
Cavallini has provided a date-linked sequence including at least a pre-op attendance window, procedure date, and expected local recovery duration.
Pass → Proceed
Treat flights, lodging, and transfer bookings as eligible for confirmation and populate all dependent rows in this inventory.
Fail → Remediate
Set affected rows to Held Pending Clinical Sequence, mark overall travel status HOLD FOR CLINICAL SEQUENCE, and escalate back to clinic scheduling coordination before any nonrefundable booking.
Decision Gate — g-02 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Escort Coverage Gate for Sedation or Major Surgery
If IV sedation, major implant surgery, or extensive same-day prosthetic work is in scope, a named escort with direct contact information and procedure-day availability is documented.
Pass → Proceed
Mark sedation-day discharge transport and first recovery-phase support as confirmed.
Fail → Remediate
Mark itinerary SEDATION ITINERARY NONCOMPLIANT and do not treat trip logistics as complete until the escort dependency is resolved or the treatment plan is restructured.
Date Completed
Reviewed By
Repeat-use worksheet · Photocopy as needed
Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 34
CAVALLINI COSTA RICA RESTORATIVE DENTAL TRAVEL CONTROL SYSTEM Chapter 4 — Passport-to-Chair Travel Execution, Sedation-Compatibl…
Worksheet CHECKLIST → Costa Rica Medical Travel and Surgical Trip Readiness

Passport Validity, Entry Document, and Sedation-Day Escort Compliance Checklist for Costa Rica Dental Travel

Verifies that legal travel readiness, return eligibility, and escort-dependent sedation compliance are complete before tickets and procedure dates are treated as operationally locked.

⏱ 25 minutes on first review, 5 minutes for pre-departure recheck ↗ See also: Costa Rica Surgical Trip Logistics Inventory for Flights, Lodging, Transfers, and Escort Coverage · Cavallini Appointment-to-Flight Timing Planner
What a verified item looks like
✓
Verified that the airline reservation name exactly matches the passport name by comparing the AA confirmation to the passport data page on 06/03/2026 — pass: both show 'EMILY RUTH CARTER' with no spacing or middle-name mismatch; no correction required.
Verify that the physical U.S. passport is in hand by checking the actual booklet, not a renewal receipt or photo copy — pass = booklet is physically available for packing today, fail = stop airfare finalization and escalate immediately to passport replacement or treatment-date movement.
Verify that the passport expiration date supports the planned Costa Rica trip window by inspecting the data page of the passport booklet — pass = expiration is clearly after the planned return date with no same-week uncertainty, fail = mark trip readiness blocked and escalate to expedited document resolution before any nonrefundable booking.
Verify that the passport is not materially damaged by examining the data page, cover, and page integrity under normal light — pass = no tearing, water damage, detached binding, or unreadable data page, fail = treat passport as noncompliant and initiate replacement rather than gambling on airline acceptance.
Verify that the airline reservation name exactly matches the passport name by comparing the booking confirmation against the passport data page character-for-character — pass = complete match including middle names or initials used by the airline, fail = correct the reservation before check-in risk is created.
Verify that the patient has the documents needed to re-enter the United States on the planned return itinerary by reviewing the passport and return booking file together — pass = same passport supports departure, entry to Costa Rica, and boarding home, fail = trip remains blocked until the return-document issue is resolved.
Verify that passport readiness remains stable within 45 days of departure by checking whether any renewal, replacement, or correction request is still pending — pass = no unresolved document process remains open, fail = escalate to expedited resolution and move the treatment date instead of assuming turnaround.
Verify that the planned return date on the itinerary still matches the passport-based travel window by comparing the latest airline confirmation to the passport expiration date — pass = return date remains within the document window, fail = revise the trip or documents before tickets are treated as final.
Verify that a digital backup of the passport identity page is stored in the travel folder and accessible from the phone without checked baggage — pass = file opens offline and is legible, fail = create the backup before departure.
Verify that the patient has a printed copy of the outbound and return itinerary in the carry-on document set by checking the physical travel folder — pass = printed itinerary is present and readable, fail = print and insert before packing is marked complete.
Verify that the Cavallini clinic has the current arrival date and contactable mobile number by checking the most recent email or WhatsApp exchange with scheduling — pass = clinic acknowledgement exists in writing, fail = send same-day operational update before travel.
Verify that the treatment plan has been reviewed for escort dependency by checking the written clinical sequence for IV sedation, major implant surgery, or extensive same-day prosthetic work — pass = escort requirement is clearly identified as yes or no, fail = request clarification from Cavallini before treating the itinerary as compliant.
Verify that a named sedation-day escort is assigned by reviewing this checklist against the travel roster — pass = one specific person is listed by full name, fail = itinerary remains noncompliant until a substitute is confirmed or sedation is restructured.
Verify that the named escort’s direct mobile number is documented and tested by calling or messaging the escort from the patient’s phone — pass = successful live contact or confirmed reply is logged, fail = obtain a working number before departure.
Verify that the escort will be physically present in San José on the procedure date by comparing their travel confirmation or local presence plan against the Cavallini surgery schedule — pass = documented overlap exists for the full discharge window, fail = treat escort coverage as absent and escalate to clinic scheduling.
Verify that the escort understands the discharge role by reviewing written discharge-support expectations with them before travel — pass = escort confirms they will accompany the patient from clinic to lodging and monitor first transport, fail = replace or brief the escort before sedation-day approval is granted.
Verify that escort lodging alignment supports immediate post-sedation transport by comparing the escort’s hotel booking or local address to the patient’s surgery-day lodging — pass = escort can realistically reach the clinic and lodging without reliance on the sedated patient, fail = revise lodging or escort plan.
Verify that a backup escort path or clinic-approved alternative has been explored if the primary escort cancels by checking written notes or clinic communication — pass = substitute path is documented, fail = elevate risk status and contact Cavallini before travel.
Verify that no escort-dependent sedation case remains unresolved within 24 hours of the scheduled procedure by reviewing the final pre-op status — pass = escort is confirmed and reachable, fail = mark the itinerary SEDATION ITINERARY NONCOMPLIANT and contact the clinic immediately.
Verify that all critical travel documents needed on arrival are assigned to carry-on rather than checked baggage by physically inspecting the carry-on packout — pass = passport, itinerary, and clinic contact sheet are in carry-on, fail = repack before airport departure.
Verify that the patient can contact the airline, hotel, escort, and Cavallini clinic if an itinerary disruption occurs by checking the phone contact list and charger availability — pass = all four contact paths are saved and accessible, fail = build the contact sheet before travel readiness is approved.
Verify that any travel disruption affecting pre-op, surgery, or discharge transport within 48 hours will trigger same-day clinic notification by reviewing the communication rule with the patient — pass = patient acknowledges the rule and clinic contact path is ready, fail = do not close this checklist until escalation steps are documented.
Verify that the current itinerary has not changed since the last escort compliance review by comparing the latest airline confirmation to the escort plan date-time entries — pass = dates still align, fail = re-run escort verification immediately.
Verify that return flight timing does not force commercial travel before discharge transport and immediate recovery needs are met by comparing the flight record against the current treatment sequence — pass = no same-day compression exists against clinic expectations, fail = rebook or extend lodging before trip approval.
Verify that this checklist has been re-reviewed after any change in arrival date, procedure date, escort, or lodging by checking the revision log or date of last review — pass = review date reflects the latest change, fail = treat compliance status as stale and reopen the checklist.
! Common Errors to Avoid
  • Assuming a valid passport alone means trip readiness without verifying exact name match to the airline reservation and physical passport condition.
  • Treating an escort as 'available' because they verbally agreed, without documenting their direct contact number, same-day physical presence, and lodging alignment for post-sedation discharge.
  • Failing to re-check document and escort status within 48 hours of departure after a schedule change, leaving stale compliance assumptions in place.
Decision Gates
Decision Gate — g-01 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Passport Readiness Gate
Passport is in hand, undamaged, matches the booking name, and supports departure to Costa Rica and return to the United States on the planned dates.
Pass → Proceed
Approve legal travel readiness and allow ticketing and departure preparation to continue.
Fail → Remediate
Stop trip execution immediately, delay airfare finalization if not yet booked, and escalate to expedited document resolution or treatment-date movement.
Decision Gate — g-02 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Sedation Escort Compliance Gate
For any IV sedation or escort-dependent surgery case, a named escort with direct contact information, procedure-day presence, and lodging alignment is confirmed no later than 24 hours before the procedure.
Pass → Proceed
Set status to SEDATION-COMPATIBLE ITINERARY CONFIRMED.
Fail → Remediate
Set status to SEDATION ITINERARY NONCOMPLIANT and escalate to Cavallini to restructure or postpone the sedation-dependent plan.
Date Completed
Reviewed By
Repeat-use worksheet · Photocopy as needed

CAVALLINI COSTA RICA RESTORATIVE DENTAL TRAVEL CONTROL SYSTEM Chapter 4 — Passport-to-Chair Travel Execution, Sedation-Compatibl…
Worksheet → Costa Rica Medical Travel and Surgical Trip Readiness

Cavallini Appointment-to-Flight Timing Planner

Maps the confirmed Cavallini clinical sequence against international travel timing so the patient can approve only itineraries with adequate disruption margin before treatment and after clinically significant events.

⏱ 40 minutes on first build, 15 minutes after each schedule revision ↗ See also: Passport Validity, Entry Document, and Sedation-Day Escort Compliance Checklist for Costa Rica Dental Travel · Costa Rica Surgical Trip Logistics Inventory for Flights, Lodging, Transfers, and Escort Coverage · San José Post-Procedure Lodging and Ground Transport Routing Form
Confirmed Cavallini Clinical Sequence Anchors
s-01
Before you start

Before completing this section, gather the latest written appointment sequence from Cavallini, including the pre-op attendance window, procedure date, estimated chair duration, any onsite lab dependencies, and the clinic’s expected local recovery period. Do not use memory or an earlier email thread if the schedule has changed. This section depends on the clinic having accepted the diagnostic records packet and issued a date-linked treatment path. This section is complete when every clinically consequential event for this trip phase is dated, timed if known, and tied to the specific treatment objective in Costa Rica.

Cavallini treatment phase being scheduled on this trip * select
All-on-X upper arch extractions, implant placement, and provisional delivery
Select: Single crown or multi-crown restorative visit / Implant placement / Implant uncovering or restorative phase / All-on-X / full-arch surgery with provisionals / Full-mouth restoration with mixed surgery and prosthetics / Other Cavallini restorative phase
Choose the actual trip phase such as 'Implant placement with IV sedation' or 'Final zirconia bridge delivery after osseointegration.'
Written Cavallini pre-op appointment date and local San José time *
06/17/2026 — 9:00 AM CST (San José)
Enter both date and local Costa Rica time exactly as provided by Cavallini.
Written Cavallini primary procedure date and planned start time *
06/18/2026 — 8:00 AM implant surgery with IV sedation
Example: '07/09/2026 — 7:30 AM full-arch surgery and immediate provisional workflow.'
Estimated chair-time duration for the main procedure as communicated by Cavallini *
Approximately 5–6 hours including sedation recovery before discharge
Record the clinic’s estimate, not your guess.
Onsite lab, prosthetic try-in, or specialist dependency that affects same-trip timing *
Provisional bridge adjustment expected on 06/19/2026; onsite lab may require same-day return if bite refinement is needed
Describe any lab-linked or specialist-linked step that could delay departure.
Clinic-stated minimum number of local recovery days after this treatment phase * number
3
Enter the numeric minimum communicated by Cavallini, such as 3 for post-op observation through Day 5.
Medication pickup and first-dose timing requirement after procedure *
Pharmacy pickup immediately after discharge; first antibiotic dose within 1 hour of hotel return
Include timing, not just the fact that medications exist.
Outbound Flight Margin and Arrival Robustness Test
s-02
Before you start

Before completing this section, gather the current outbound airline itinerary, connection times, expected baggage plan, and the route from Juan Santamaría International Airport to the clinic-area lodging. Review failure scenarios from this chapter, especially immigration delay, baggage delay, and traffic into San José. This section depends on the clinical anchors in Section 1 being complete. This section is complete when the chosen arrival plan can absorb routine disruption without causing loss of the first required Cavallini event.

Selected outbound flight path from U.S. origin airport to SJO *
Austin (AUS) → Dallas/Fort Worth (DFW) → San José (SJO) on American Airlines
List the full route, not just the final destination.
Scheduled touchdown time in San José for the chosen arrival itinerary *
06/16/2026 — 1:55 PM CST (San José)
Use local arrival time in Costa Rica.
Disruption buffer between scheduled SJO arrival and first required Cavallini event *
19 hours 5 minutes between touchdown and 06/17/2026 9:00 AM pre-op
Express as elapsed time, not as 'arrive day before.'
Would a delay of four hours or less cause loss of the first clinic event * boolean
False means the itinerary survives a routine disruption; true means the itinerary fails robustness review.
Arrival compression risk classification for this Cavallini trip phase * select
Low risk — arrival day before pre-op with hotel check-in margin
Select: Low risk — full buffer before first clinic event / Moderate risk — manageable but sensitive to one disruption / High risk — same-day arrival or narrow margin / Unacceptable — one routine delay breaks the sequence
Choose the risk level that matches the actual timeline margin.
Decision on same-day arrival before chair time for this treatment phase * select
Rejected for major implant surgery due to fatigue and immigration-delay risk
Select: Rejected — major restorative case requires pre-treatment overnight / Accepted only with explicit Cavallini instruction / Accepted for low-intensity restorative phase / REQUIRES CLARIFICATION
Record whether same-day arrival is accepted or rejected and why.
Rebooking trigger if arrival margin becomes non-robust *
If any schedule change reduces buffer below one full disruption window, move to earlier inbound flight or add pre-treatment hotel night and notify Cavallini same day
State the exact trigger and corrective action.
Post-Procedure Local Stay and Return Flight Clearance Logic
s-03
Before you start

Before completing this section, gather the current return itinerary, the clinic’s stated observation period, expected follow-up check timing, and any known provisional or bite-adjustment appointments. Review hotel booking flexibility and airfare change rules alongside the treatment schedule. This section depends on Section 1 and Section 2 being complete because departure can only be judged against a real procedure timeline. This section is complete when the return flight is shown to occur after all clinically significant events and after the planned local recovery window, with an extension path documented if recovery runs long.

Scheduled return flight departure from SJO *
06/21/2026 — 12:40 PM SJO to DFW
Use the booked departure from Costa Rica, not the final U.S. arrival time.
Last clinically significant Cavallini event before airport travel *
06/20/2026 — 10:00 AM post-op review and provisional bite adjustment
Examples include post-op review, medication reconciliation, bite check, discharge clearance, or prosthetic delivery.
Elapsed buffer between last clinically significant event and airport departure *
26 hours 40 minutes
Calculate the time gap; do not write 'next day.'
Flexible lodging extension capacity if Cavallini requests additional local observation *
