! Common Errors to Avoid
  • Recording only one pain score per day instead of morning/mid-day/evening/bedtime intervals during the first 48 hours after implants, extractions, or All-on-X treatment, which hides worsening trends before the return flight.
  • Copying the prescribed medication schedule from the Cavallini discharge sheet instead of logging the doses actually taken, delayed, vomited, or skipped, making symptom interpretation unreliable.
  • Writing 'swelling normal' without noting side, spread, and functional effect; unilateral expansion or swallowing difficulty can require same-day reassessment in San José.
Decision Gates
Decision Gate — g-06-a-01 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Controlled Recovery Monitoring Active
Discharge instructions, medication list, urgent contact route, and procedure details from Cavallini are present before the first recovery entry is made.
Pass → Proceed
Begin scheduled symptom and medication logging immediately on arrival at hotel or recovery lodging.
Fail → Remediate
Stop recovery logging setup and obtain missing packet items from Cavallini before treating the stay as actively monitored.
Decision Gate — g-06-a-02 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Trend Reversal Escalation Threshold
Any major variable worsens for two consecutive monitoring intervals, or pain remains 7/10 or higher for more than two consecutive intervals despite medication taken as directed.
Pass → Proceed
Move the issue from observation into the contact lane and update ws-06-b and ws-06-c with the escalation taken.
Fail → Remediate
Continue the scheduled monitoring cadence only if symptoms are stable or improving.
Date Completed
Reviewed By
Repeat-use worksheet · Photocopy as needed
Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 50
CAVALLINI COSTA RICA RESTORATIVE DENTAL TRAVEL CONTROL SYSTEM Chapter 6 — Post-Operative Symptom Surveillance, Medication Adhere…
Worksheet CHECKLIST → US Reentry Continuity of Care and Cavallini Warranty Preservation

Post-Implant and Full-Mouth Surgery Red-Flag Escalation Checklist for San José Stay

Produces a documented escalation decision showing whether symptoms remain in self-monitoring, require same-day clinic contact, need urgent reassessment, or justify emergency intervention before any flight proceeds.

⏱ 20 minutes on first setup, 3-5 minutes per symptom event review ↗ See also: Costa Rica Dental Recovery Daily Symptom and Medication Tracking Log · Clinic Contact, Local Emergency Option, and Escalation Routing Form for Post-Op Issues · Return-Flight Fitness Assessment After Cavallini Restorative Treatment
What a verified item looks like
✓
Verified that pain remained below the escalation threshold by reviewing ws-06-a entries from 8:00 AM and 2:00 PM on 2026-02-14 — pass = pain fell from 5/10 to 3/10 after scheduled ibuprofen and no extra rescue dosing was needed; no clinic contact required.
Verify that the exact Cavallini discharge instructions for bleeding, swelling, rinsing, diet stage, sleeping position, and activity limits are physically available at the recovery lodging by checking the paper packet, clinic email, or WhatsApp attachment — pass = the instructions can be read without contacting the clinic again; fail = request the missing instruction set from Cavallini before relying on self-monitoring.
Verify that ws-06-a contains the procedure performed, the local time anesthesia or IV sedation ended, the first pain score, and the first oral intake by reviewing the first row of the recovery log — pass = all four baseline items are recorded; fail = reconstruct the baseline immediately with the patient, escort, and discharge paperwork before symptoms are interpreted.
Verify that every prescribed medication from Cavallini is identified by name, strength, dose interval, and start timing using the onsite pharmacy label or written prescription — pass = each medication can be matched to an actual package or written order; fail = contact the clinic or pharmacy before the next dose window.
Verify that current pain has been compared across at least two monitoring intervals in ws-06-a rather than judged from a single moment — pass = trend direction is visible; fail = continue logging until trend data exist unless another emergency threshold is already met.
Verify whether pain remains uncontrolled at 7/10 or higher for more than two consecutive monitoring intervals despite prescribed medication being taken as directed by checking ws-06-a dose entries and pain scores — pass = no such pattern exists or it has already been escalated; fail = contact Cavallini the same day for reassessment instructions and suspend travel-preparation tasks.
Verify whether active bleeding continues beyond the clinic's expected window after pressure measures by checking gauze-change frequency, pooling, and restart episodes in ws-06-a against the discharge instructions — pass = bleeding is light and decreasing; fail = seek immediate clinic guidance and do not continue routine departure planning.
Verify whether swelling is stabilizing or declining rather than rapidly extending, becoming one-sided, or interfering with swallowing by comparing check-in notes and companion observations — pass = swelling follows expected early-peak behavior; fail = move to same-day clinic contact or urgent reassessment based on functional impact.
Verify whether fever has reached 100.4 F or higher after the initial recovery window, especially when paired with increasing swelling, foul taste, chills, or malaise, by checking actual thermometer readings and symptom notes — pass = temperature remains below threshold or clearly improving; fail = treat as possible infection pathway and request urgent reassessment before any flight segment proceeds.
Verify whether the patient can drink adequate fluids and keep medications down for at least 12 hours after expected sedation recovery using the medication log, oral intake notes, and nausea/vomiting entries — pass = hydration and medication continuity are intact; fail = contact Cavallini because recovery control is broken and flight readiness will likely fail.
Verify whether any antibiotic, analgesic, anti-inflammatory, or mouth rinse was skipped, delayed by more than 2 hours, or vomited soon after dosing by reviewing ws-06-a medication events — pass = adherence is intact or deviation has been reviewed by the clinic; fail = document the event clearly and contact Cavallini if symptoms are worsening or repeated doses are affected.
Verify whether the patient can ambulate safely to the bathroom and within the hotel without instability by direct observation from the escort or by a self-check if no sedation was used — pass = mobility is safe for basic recovery needs; fail = treat return-flight clearance as not met and consider urgent reassessment if decline is new.
Verify whether IV sedation was used and, if so, whether an escort completed the first-night viability check for swallowing medication, confusion level, overnight dosing, and bathroom safety by reviewing escort notes and ws-06-a — pass = escort verification exists; fail = document the gap and increase direct monitoring immediately.
Verify whether oral intake matches the diet stage specified by Cavallini rather than patient experimentation by checking the discharge sheet and meal notes — pass = intake follows the permitted stage; fail = stop unauthorized foods, document the deviation, and reassess symptoms for behavior-linked worsening.
Verify whether the patient used a straw, smoked, drank alcohol, rinsed vigorously too early, or tested solid food against instructions by asking directly and reviewing notes — pass = no prohibited behavior occurred or it was reported promptly; fail = document the deviation and contact Cavallini if pain, bleeding, or prosthetic symptoms worsened afterward.
Verify whether swallowing water causes severe pain, choking concern, or functional obstruction by testing only within clinic instructions and recording the result — pass = swallowing is possible with controlled discomfort; fail = move out of self-monitoring and seek urgent guidance.
Verify whether mouth opening is narrowing compared with the previous check-in, especially when paired with unilateral swelling, by comparing ws-06-a entries and direct observation — pass = opening is stable or improving; fail = same-day clinic contact or urgent reassessment depending on severity.
Verify whether the provisional full-arch, temporary bridge, or fresh crown work has developed a sudden high spot, clicking, shifting sensation, or localized pain on closure by patient report and gentle instructed function only — pass = no mechanical warning sign is present; fail = stop testing the prosthesis and request evaluation before airport day.
Verify whether the patient is waking due to escalating pain or requiring rescue pain medication at intervals shorter than prescribed by reviewing overnight log entries and pill timing — pass = medication spacing matches the prescription and sleep disruption is limited; fail = request clinic input before self-clearing for travel.
Verify whether bleeding, pain, swelling, hydration, urination, medication completion, overnight contact ability, and bathroom mobility were all checked before sleep on post-op day 0 and day 1 using ws-06-a and escort notes — pass = all viability items were reviewed; fail = perform the viability check immediately and escalate any deficit.
Verify whether the clinic contact route for after-hours issues is known and reachable from the hotel by checking ws-06-c phone numbers, messaging apps, and front-desk assistance options — pass = at least one active overnight route is confirmed; fail = obtain the contact path before the next overnight interval.
Verify whether the patient still appears to be improving overall rather than deteriorating after an earlier brief improvement by comparing the last three logged intervals — pass = trend remains stable or improving; fail = treat delayed deterioration as operationally significant and contact Cavallini.
Verify whether any red-flag cluster is present: fever plus increasing swelling plus worsening pain, or uncontrolled nausea plus missed medications plus poor hydration, using ws-06-a entries — pass = no cluster exists; fail = urgent reassessment is indicated before travel proceeds.
Verify whether emergency fallback beyond Cavallini has been identified, including local transport and emergency facility routing in San José, by reviewing ws-06-c — pass = fallback is documented and usable; fail = complete ws-06-c immediately because delay during an acute event is unacceptable.
Verify whether routine self-monitoring remains appropriate by checking that bleeding is decreasing, pain is controlled, fluids are tolerated, and ambulation is safe — pass = stay in self-monitoring lane with scheduled checks; fail = move to the appropriate higher lane and document the transition.
Verify whether any unresolved item above the self-monitoring threshold remains open at the end of the day by reviewing this checklist and the action notes in ws-06-a — pass = every higher-level item has documented resolution or active clinician instruction; fail = the domain cannot close and travel clearance is blocked.
! Common Errors to Avoid
  • Treating this as a comfort checklist instead of a decision threshold tool; the point is to classify worsening bleeding, swelling, fever, prosthetic instability, or medication failure before airport day.
  • Using anxiety or reassurance from travel companions as the escalation standard instead of the actual Cavallini discharge thresholds, trend pattern, and functional decline.
  • Boarding a return flight with an unresolved high-bite or shifting provisional because nothing is visibly broken; mechanical instability in All-on-X cases must be escalated while still in San José.
Decision Gates
Decision Gate — g-06-b-01 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Self-Monitoring vs Escalation Classification
No unresolved item exceeds routine self-monitoring thresholds; worsening variables have not persisted across two consecutive check-ins; no red-flag cluster is present.
Pass → Proceed
Remain in routine monitoring and continue scheduled logging in ws-06-a.
Fail → Remediate
Escalate to same-day clinic contact, urgent reassessment, or emergency intervention using ws-06-c.
Decision Gate — g-06-b-02 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Travel Preparation Suspension Trigger
Active bleeding persists beyond expected window, fever/infection cluster appears, hydration or medication continuity breaks, or mobility fails for safe transport.
Pass → Proceed
Travel-preparation tasks may continue only if all thresholds are cleared.
Fail → Remediate
Suspend packing, checkout, and airport transfer planning until Cavallini or emergency stabilization resolves the issue.
Date Completed
Reviewed By
Repeat-use worksheet · Photocopy as needed
Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 51
CAVALLINI COSTA RICA RESTORATIVE DENTAL TRAVEL CONTROL SYSTEM Chapter 6 — Post-Operative Symptom Surveillance, Medication Adhere…
Worksheet → US Reentry Continuity of Care and Cavallini Warranty Preservation

Clinic Contact, Local Emergency Option, and Escalation Routing Form for Post-Op Issues

Builds the live communication stack needed to reach Cavallini, hotel support, transportation, and emergency fallback quickly when post-operative symptoms worsen in San José.

⏱ 30 minutes on first use, 5 minutes for updates ↗ See also: Costa Rica Dental Recovery Daily Symptom and Medication Tracking Log · Post-Implant and Full-Mouth Surgery Red-Flag Escalation Checklist for San José Stay
Cavallini Post-Operative Contact Hierarchy
s-06-c-01
Before you start

Before completing this section, gather the Cavallini discharge packet, any WhatsApp messages or email confirmations from the clinic, and the medication handoff paperwork from procedure day. You should also have ws-06-a open so the exact procedure and recovery date can be copied consistently into any escalation message. Complete this section before the first night in the hotel, especially if IV sedation, implants, extractions, bone grafting, or All-on-X treatment was performed. This section is complete when at least one daytime contact path and one after-hours path are recorded with a tested or confirmed method of use.

Primary Cavallini daytime post-op contact name and role for recovery questions *
Ana Rodríguez — International Patient Coordinator
Enter a named person or role used for post-op routing, such as 'Ana Rodríguez — International Patient Coordinator'.
Direct daytime phone or WhatsApp number actually provided for post-treatment issues *
+506 8888-2147 (WhatsApp enabled)
Record the direct number exactly as dialed from Costa Rica, e.g. '+506 8888-2147 (WhatsApp enabled)'.
After-hours Cavallini escalation route for bleeding, fever, swelling, or uncontrolled pain *
After-hours post-op WhatsApp monitored by on-call dental team: +506 8701-4422
Specify the route and use method, such as 'After-hours post-op WhatsApp monitored by on-call dental team: +506 8701-4422'.
Preferred message format requested by Cavallini when reporting a complication *
Send: patient full name, procedure date, hotel name/room, symptom timeline, temperature, pain score, photos if swelling visible, medication doses already taken
A complete entry names the exact information the clinic expects in the first message, not a generic note like 'text them if needed'.
Exact Cavallini procedure description to include in every escalation message *
Upper All-on-6 with 4 extractions, IV sedation, immediate provisional delivered on 2026-02-12
State the actual procedure and date, e.g. 'Implant at #19 with bone graft on 2026-02-14'.
Companion or escort assigned to communicate if the patient is sedated, nauseated, or unable to explain symptoms clearly
Michael Costa — spouse and first-night recorder — U.S. mobile +1 617-555-0184
If an escort is present, record full identity and number, such as 'Michael Costa — spouse — +1 617-555-0184'.
Recovery Lodging and Local Movement Control
s-06-c-02
Before you start

Before completing this section, retrieve the hotel confirmation, room assignment, front desk phone card, airport-transfer booking, and any clinic-arranged transport details. If the clinic's onsite hotel or preferred lodging changed after surgery, use the actual current location, not the original reservation. This section must be completed before the first overnight period because hotel staff or drivers may need to help route urgent reassessment. This section is complete when a third party could locate you and arrange transport without asking follow-up questions.

Current San José recovery lodging name, full address, and room number *
Holiday Inn Express Forum Costa Rica — Del Centro Empresarial Forum 2, Santa Ana — Room 314
Include enough detail for pickup, e.g. 'Hotel name — street/location — room number'.
Hotel front desk number and whether English-language assistance is available overnight *
+506 2208-2100 — overnight English support available
Record both number and language support, such as '+506 2208-2100 — overnight English support available'.
Fastest transport option back to Cavallini for same-day reassessment from this lodging *
Uber from hotel main entrance to Cavallini clinic, approx. 12 minutes outside rush hour
Name the actual transport method and pickup point, not just 'taxi'.
Airport transfer provider and cancellation cutoff if departure must be postponed for medical reasons
Clinic-arranged shuttle through Interbus — must cancel 4 hours before pickup to avoid charge
A complete entry includes provider name and cutoff, such as 'Interbus — cancel 4 hours before pickup'.
Stair, elevator, or mobility limitation at current lodging that could affect departure-day safety *
Room on 3rd floor; elevator available; no stairs required to reach lobby pickup area
State the real movement condition, e.g. 'One flight of stairs to breakfast area; elevator to lobby available'.
Nearest pharmacy or hotel assistance source if replacement gauze, ice, bottled water, or soft-food support is needed overnight
Hotel front desk can send bottled water/ice; Farmacia Fischel at Momentum Pinares closes 10 PM
Record a specific support source, such as 'Hotel front desk can deliver ice and extra bottled water overnight'.
Emergency Fallback Outside the Planned Treatment Chain
s-06-c-03
Before you start

Before completing this section, review the discharge instructions for any emergency directions from Cavallini, confirm local emergency numbers usable from your Costa Rica phone or roaming setup, and note whether your travel companion can communicate in Spanish. Do not wait until symptoms accelerate to research emergency fallback. If this section cannot be completed with verified details, mark REQUIRES CLARIFICATION and obtain the information from the clinic or hotel immediately. This section is complete when both urgent clinic reassessment and true emergency care routes are documented separately.

Costa Rica emergency number to call from local mobile or hotel line if airway, uncontrolled bleeding, or collapse occurs *
911 from Costa Rica mobile or hotel phone
Enter the actual emergency number and call context, e.g. '911 from Costa Rica mobile or hotel phone'.
Nearest emergency facility or hospital the hotel or Cavallini would use if post-op symptoms exceed clinic scope *
Hospital CIMA San José — Autopista Próspero Fernández, Escazú
A correct entry names a real facility and location, such as 'Hospital CIMA San José — Escazú'.
Emergency transport fallback if rideshare is unavailable or the patient cannot sit upright safely *
Hotel front desk to call licensed taxi immediately; ambulance via 911 if airway concern or uncontrolled bleeding
State both non-emergency and emergency transport fallback, not just 'call a cab'.
Spanish-language phrase or hotel assistance note for reporting 'recent dental implants/full-mouth surgery with swelling, bleeding, fever, or medication problems'
Necesito ayuda médica. Tuve cirugía dental con implantes ayer y ahora tengo hinchazón, sangrado y fiebre.
Use a phrase that directly describes the dental-surgery situation, e.g. 'Tuve cirugía dental ayer y ahora tengo sangrado que no para'.
U.S. emergency contact who should be updated if reassessment, hospitalization, or flight delay occurs *
Laura Benton — daughter — +1 404-555-0171 — update if overnight observation or flight change needed
Record full name, relationship, number, and update trigger.
Rapid Report Packet for Any Escalation Call or Message
s-06-c-04
Before you start

Before completing this section, open ws-06-a and copy the most recent symptom data, medication timing, temperature, and oral-intake status. You will also need the pharmacy labels, the discharge packet, and any photos of swelling, bleeding, or prosthesis concerns taken during the stay. This section should be updated every time a significant symptom changes because it becomes the script for contacting Cavallini or emergency services. This section is complete when another person could send an accurate escalation message using only the entries below.

Most recent temperature, pain score, and exact local time ready to report verbatim *
100.5 F, pain 7/10 at 2:00 PM San José time
Use the real measured values, e.g. '98.9 F, pain 4/10 at 8:00 AM'.
Current bleeding, swelling, and swallowing status statement for first-line triage *
Bleeding no longer soaking gauze, but right-side facial swelling increased since morning; swallowing water is uncomfortable but possible
Provide a concise triage statement covering the core oral issue, not a vague phrase like 'feels worse'.
Last three Cavallini medication doses actually taken, delayed, or vomited *
1:00 PM ibuprofen 600 mg taken; 12:30 PM Augmentin delayed 2 hrs due to nausea; 8:00 AM dexamethasone taken with yogurt
List actual dose events in sequence, such as '8:00 AM clindamycin taken; 12:00 PM acetaminophen vomited 20 min later'.
Hydration, urination, and soft-food tolerance status for the last 12 hours *
Approx. 24 oz water tolerated, urinated twice, soup caused nausea, yogurt tolerated
A complete entry addresses all three variables: fluids, urination, and food tolerance.
Photo or video evidence location for swelling, bleeding, or prosthesis problem
iPhone album 'Cavallini recovery' — photos taken 2:05 PM and 4:40 PM showing right facial swelling
Identify exactly where the evidence is stored and when captured.
Go-to escalation script to send first if symptoms worsen after hours *
This is Julia Benton, upper All-on-6 on 2026-02-12. At 2:00 PM I have 100.5 F fever, pain 7/10 despite medication, and right-side swelling increasing since morning. Staying at Holiday Inn Express Forum, Room 314. Please advise if I should return to clinic now.
The script should include identity, procedure/date, current symptoms, lodging location, and request for instruction.
! Common Errors to Avoid
  • Saving only the general clinic website or main reception number without confirming the actual after-hours or urgent post-op route used during the San José recovery stay.
  • Failing to write down the exact hotel, room, and transport pickup details, which delays reassessment when swelling, bleeding, or sedation-related problems make verbal explanation difficult.
  • Leaving medication names in unlabeled packaging from the onsite pharmacy without transcribing them into this routing form; emergency clinicians or drivers may need the list immediately.
Decision Gates
Decision Gate — g-06-c-01 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Escalation Routing Ready Before First Overnight Stay
Daytime clinic route, after-hours route, lodging location, and emergency fallback are all documented and usable during the stay.
Pass → Proceed
Treat communication readiness as active and use this form for any post-op event.
Fail → Remediate
Do not rely on memory; complete missing routing items immediately with the clinic or hotel before overnight recovery continues.
Decision Gate — g-06-c-02 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Report Packet Ready for Rapid Reassessment Request
A third party could accurately report the procedure, symptoms, medications, hotel location, and callback route using the completed form.
Pass → Proceed
Use the rapid report packet for all calls, WhatsApp messages, or emergency desk interactions.
Fail → Remediate
Update the missing fields before symptoms worsen further or transport is initiated.
Date Completed
Reviewed By
Repeat-use worksheet · Photocopy as needed
Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 52
CAVALLINI COSTA RICA RESTORATIVE DENTAL TRAVEL CONTROL SYSTEM Chapter 6 — Post-Operative Symptom Surveillance, Medication Adhere…
Worksheet → US Reentry Continuity of Care and Cavallini Warranty Preservation

Return-Flight Fitness Assessment After Cavallini Restorative Treatment

Produces the go/no-go decision on whether the patient is clinically and operationally fit to complete commercial travel from San José back to the United States after Cavallini treatment.

⏱ 20-30 minutes on departure-evaluation day ↗ See also: Costa Rica Dental Recovery Daily Symptom and Medication Tracking Log · Post-Implant and Full-Mouth Surgery Red-Flag Escalation Checklist for San José Stay · Clinic Contact, Local Emergency Option, and Escalation Routing Form for Post-Op Issues
Departure-Day Symptom Stability and Function Screen
s-06-d-01
Before you start

Before completing this section, review the last 24 to 48 hours of ws-06-a entries, the results of ws-06-b, and the Cavallini discharge instructions or any updated reassessment notes. Do not complete this section from memory; use measured temperature, actual pain scores, and direct observations from the current day. If the patient had implants, extractions, bone-related work, or All-on-X treatment, compare the present state against the trend, not just this morning's comfort level. This section is complete when each travel-critical symptom is documented as improving, stable, or unacceptable for airport transit.

Planned departure date and airline first-segment departure time from San José *
2026-02-15 — United 1087 departing SJO at 1:20 PM
Record the actual departure timing, e.g. '2026-02-15 — Delta 1742 departing SJO at 1:20 PM'.
Current pain score and whether control is predictable through a 6- to 10-hour airport plus flight block *
Pain 3/10 at rest, 4/10 with speech; controlled on current schedule and no breakthrough dosing needed since midnight
A correct entry states score plus travel-block predictability, not just 'pain manageable'.
Current bleeding status at all surgical or restorative sites on departure morning *
No active bleeding; occasional faint pink saliva only; no fresh gauze needed overnight
Describe the real bleeding state, e.g. 'No active bleeding; gauze not required since 9 PM'.
Current swelling trend and any effect on swallowing, speech, or mouth opening *
Upper facial swelling slightly improved vs yesterday; swallowing water and speaking in short sentences without difficulty
Include trend and function, such as 'Left mandibular swelling stable; swallowing intact; mouth opening still limited to two fingers'.
Most recent temperature and whether any fever-plus-swelling-plus-pain cluster remains unresolved *
98.7 F at 7:30 AM; no unresolved fever cluster
Use measured value plus cluster status, e.g. '100.4 F at 6:45 AM; unresolved fever cluster present'.
Departure-day oral function status for sipping water, taking medication, and tolerating approved soft food *
Able to sip water, swallow tablets, and eat yogurt/oatmeal without severe pain or nausea
Address all three functions: fluids, medications, and diet tolerance.
Medication Continuity and Travel-Day Dosing Control
s-06-d-02
Before you start

Before completing this section, gather the pharmacy labels from Cavallini or the onsite pharmacy, the actual pill supply being packed into carry-on luggage, and the last dose entries from ws-06-a. If any medication was delayed, vomited, or skipped in the last 24 hours, note that here because it changes the reliability of symptom control in transit. This section is complete when the next required doses are timed against airport security, boarding, and the first flight segment without guesswork. Do not mark this section complete if the patient is still requiring rescue dosing more often than prescribed.

Next required antibiotic, anti-inflammatory, analgesic, or rinse dose times during airport and first flight segment *
Augmentin due 2:00 PM in terminal/aircraft; ibuprofen due 1:30 PM before boarding; rinse next due after hotel checkout not during transit
List each relevant medication with an actual due time during travel, not 'take as needed'.
Are all remaining Cavallini medications packed in carry-on with labels and enough supply for delays * boolean
Mark true only if labeled medication supply is in carry-on and sufficient for delays or missed connections.
Any medication event in the last 24 hours that makes symptom interpretation unreliable for travel clearance * select
Select: Dose delayed more than 2 hours / Dose skipped entirely / Dose vomited soon after taking / Rescue pain medication needed more often than prescribed / Multiple medication-control problems in last 24 hours
Choose the actual event status, e.g. 'Vomited dose within 30 minutes'.
Nausea, sedation effect, or dizziness status relevant to airport walking, check-in, and security screening *
No current nausea; no dizziness when standing; no residual sedation concerns
Describe the actual transport effect, e.g. 'Mild dizziness on standing; requires escort during all walking'.
Does current medication use still require an escort to manage transit safely * boolean
Mark true if the patient cannot reliably self-manage medications, mobility, or decision-making during travel.
Airport Transit Tolerance and Mobility Readiness
s-06-d-03
Before you start

Before completing this section, review the planned transport from hotel to airport, expected standing and walking requirements, luggage burden, and whether wheelchair assistance is already arranged. Use the current physical condition, not the original itinerary assumptions. If the patient cannot safely walk to the bathroom, enter a vehicle, sit upright for extended periods, or handle delays without destabilizing recovery, this section should fail even if oral symptoms are improving. This section is complete when the actual airport path has been matched to the patient's present function.

Can the patient walk from hotel room to lobby, enter transport, and clear airport processing without unsafe instability * select
Yes independently
Select: Yes independently / Yes with escort support / No — wheelchair needed / No — not fit for airport transit
Choose the real mobility level, e.g. 'Yes with escort support' or 'No — wheelchair needed'.
Estimated time the patient must remain upright from hotel checkout to first in-flight seated rest * number
4
Enter the estimated number of hours, e.g. '4'.
Hydration and restroom access plan for the full San José airport plus first-flight segment *
Carry two bottled waters after security, aisle seat assigned, restroom use expected before boarding and once during flight
A complete entry addresses fluids and bathroom access across the actual travel block.
Special airport assistance already confirmed if swelling, weakness, or oral surgery limits normal travel pace
Wheelchair assistance requested with United for SJO check-in to gate; escort traveling alongside
Record the actual service arranged, e.g. 'Wheelchair assistance confirmed through airline reservation'.
What specific condition would force same-day flight postponement instead of proceeding to the airport *
Any restart of active bleeding, fever 100.4 F or higher with increasing swelling, or inability to take noon antibiotic before departure
Name concrete no-fly triggers tied to this case, not a vague phrase like 'if worse'.
Final Return Packet Audit and Departure Decision
s-06-d-04
Before you start

Before completing this section, retrieve the full discharge packet from Cavallini, any implant or prosthetic details provided, medication records, procedure summary, and written complication instructions. You should also have ws-06-c available so the clinic and emergency contact routes can be duplicated into the return materials if needed. Do not mark the chapter complete until the packet is duplicated before leaving San José. This section is complete when the return packet would allow a U.S. dentist to triage the case without guessing what procedure, components, or medications were involved.

Are procedure summary, written post-op instructions, and complication guidance physically or digitally duplicated before airport departure * boolean
Mark true only after a duplicate copy exists outside the original packet.
Implant brand, component, or prosthetic detail included in the packet if applicable to this Cavallini case
Nobel Biocare upper arch implants with immediate provisional; component sheet printed and saved to phone
If applicable, record the exact brand/detail present, e.g. 'Zimmer implant details attached to discharge packet'.
Final medication list with actual-use notes ready for U.S. follow-up handoff *
Augmentin started 2026-02-12, one dose delayed 2 hrs on 2026-02-13 due to nausea; ibuprofen used through departure day; chlorhexidine started evening after surgery
A complete entry includes actual-use notes, not just the prescribed names.
Overall return-flight clearance decision for this departure date * select
Cleared for commercial travel
Select: Cleared for commercial travel / Conditionally cleared only with escort/support / Not cleared — Cavallini reassessment required before flight / Not cleared — emergency or urgent stabilization required
Select the actual departure decision reached after completing all sections.
If not fully cleared, exact action to take before any airport segment proceeds *
Contact Cavallini at 8:00 AM for reassessment of unilateral swelling and postpone shuttle pickup until clinician confirms travel safety
State the next operational step with timing, e.g. 'Return to clinic before checkout; cancel airport transfer by 9:00 AM if not cleared'.
! Common Errors to Avoid
  • Clearing travel because the patient feels better in bed at the hotel, without testing whether pain control, hydration, mobility, and medication timing will hold through a 6- to 10-hour airport-and-flight block.
  • Ignoring unresolved bleeding or a fresh fever because flights and airport transfers are already booked; in this domain, travel bookings do not override medical instability.
  • Checking out without auditing the final return packet for implant brand, prosthetic details, and written complication instructions, which can break U.S. handoff and warranty preservation even if the flight itself is tolerated.
Decision Gates
Decision Gate — g-06-d-01 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Commercial Travel Symptom Stability Gate
Pain is controlled, bleeding is stable, swelling is stable or improving, hydration and soft intake are adequate, and no unresolved fever/infection cluster is present.
Pass → Proceed
Proceed to medication and mobility review for final flight clearance.
Fail → Remediate
Do not self-clear for travel; remain in Costa Rica recovery control and obtain Cavallini reassessment or emergency stabilization.
Decision Gate — g-06-d-02 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Airport Mobility and Medication Continuity Gate
The patient can complete airport transit safely, carry-on medications are available and timed for travel, and no recent medication-control failure makes symptom stability unreliable.
Pass → Proceed
Approve departure logistics if return packet is also complete.
Fail → Remediate
Postpone departure or arrange additional support; do not proceed to airport on assumption alone.
Decision Gate — g-06-d-03 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Final Return Packet Gate
Procedure summary, instructions, medication record, complication guidance, and implant/prosthetic details when applicable have been duplicated before leaving San José.
Pass → Proceed
Close the chapter and trigger U.S. Follow-Up Continuity, Warranty Preservation, and Long-Term Maintenance Control.
Fail → Remediate
Halt checkout and obtain the missing documentation from Cavallini before airport departure.
Date Completed
Reviewed By
Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 53
07
Chapter 07

domain-07

Stateside Dentist Handoff, 30-Day Complication Capture, Cavallini Warranty Compliance, and Maintenance Scheduling

Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 5 control domains
Chapter 7 | domain-07

Stateside Dentist Handoff, 30-Day Complication Capture, Cavallini Warranty Compliance, and Maintenance Scheduling

What this section is for
This chapter converts completed Costa Rica treatment into a controlled U.S. follow-up system so recent restorative work remains clinically supported, documented, and warranty-defensible after you return home.
When it matters
It begins within 72 hours before departure from Costa Rica, intensifies during the first 30 days after return, and remains active for all future hygiene, imaging, prosthetic review, and warranty-related events.
Failure looks like
You land in the United States with implants, crowns, provisionals, or All-on-X work but no confirmed U.S. provider, no readable implant or prosthetic records, no symptom log, and no proof of maintenance or clinic notification if problems emerge.
This chapter produces
  • Confirmed U.S. follow-up provider arrangement with defined role and first review window
  • Complete post-return dental handoff packet with duplicate storage and readability confirmation
  • First-30-days complication surveillance log with escalation history
  • Cavallini warranty compliance and claim-evidence register
  • Long-term cleaning, imaging, and prosthetic review schedule tied to delivered treatment
Minimum Viable Actions (Start Here)
Action 1
Confirm one named U.S. dentist, oral surgeon, prosthodontist, or restorative office before the return flight and document what they agreed to do.
Action 2
Use the Post-Return Dental Handoff Packet Completeness Checklist before leaving Costa Rica to verify implant brand, procedure dates, prosthetic details, medications, instructions, images, and warranty terms are included.
Action 3
Within 48 hours of arriving home, send the packet to the U.S. provider and obtain written acknowledgement that the files were received and can be opened.
Action 4
Schedule the first U.S. review within 7 to 14 days after return for implant, implant-supported bridge, or All-on-X cases unless Cavallini gave a different timing instruction.
Action 5
Use the First-30-Days Issue Capture Form daily for days 1 through 7, then every 3 days through day 30, and immediately whenever a new symptom appears.
Action 6
Log every maintenance action, provider contact, symptom report, and evidence file in the Cavallini warranty register.
Orientation Snapshot

Orientation Snapshot

This domain begins before the return flight, not after it. Its job is to prevent a common post-tourism failure pattern: the patient lands back in the United States with recent implant surgery, crowns, provisional bridges, or an All-on-X prosthesis, but no committed U.S. provider, no organized implant documentation, and no structured process for detecting early problems. In that condition, minor occlusion issues, loose components, pressure spots, soft-tissue irritation, or hygiene breakdown can become emergency events with unclear responsibility and no clean warranty path.

The operational stake is high because Cavallini treatment is not complete when the patient leaves San José; it is only transferred. The transfer must include a U.S. follow-up provider, a complete clinical packet, a 30-day issue capture process, a warranty evidence trail, and a maintenance schedule tied to the actual restorative work delivered. If any one of those elements is missing, continuity of care becomes reactive and expensive, and the patient may face refusal of local treatment, uncovered repairs, or a repeat trip to Costa Rica.

This chapter therefore defines the control system for post-return stabilization. The primary outputs are a confirmed U.S. dentist follow-up arrangement, a complete handoff packet, a first-30-days complication surveillance log, a Cavallini warranty compliance register, and a recurring maintenance calendar. The chapter is operationally successful only when the patient can prove who will see them in the U.S., what records were transferred, what symptoms were monitored, what warranty conditions apply, and when each future surveillance event is due.

Decision threshold: if no U.S. dentist has affirmatively agreed to evaluate or triage the case before the final return home, the system is not in a stable state. Downstream effect: the patient must remain in active provider search and cannot treat the Costa Rica phase as operationally closed, even i

…

Quick Reference Rules
→ Do not treat the Costa Rica phase as complete until a U.S. provider has affirmatively accepted a role in your follow-up — Rationale: post-return instability often appears after travel, not before it. Trigger: within 72 hours before departure and again before final return home. Risk: unmanaged complications, treatment delay, and refusal of local evaluation.
→ Do not leave Costa Rica without a duplicated handoff packet containing implant system details, prosthetic records, medications, instructions, dates, and provider names — Rationale: U.S. offices cannot safely triage undocumented foreign restorative work. Trigger: final checkout after surgery, provisional delivery, or final prosthetic delivery. Risk: cross-border records chase, delayed intervention, and warranty ambiguity.
→ Treat unreadable files or missing implant data as a control failure, not a minor admin problem — Rationale: platform and component details affect local intervention scope. Trigger: when the U.S. office reports imaging cannot be opened or records are incomplete. Risk: duplicate diagnostics, delayed mechanical care, and inability to verify compatibility.
→ For implant, implant-supported bridge, or All-on-X cases, anchor the first U.S. review to a scheduled date within 7 to 14 days after return unless Cavallini directs otherwise — Rationale: bite problems, loosening, and soft-tissue issues often emerge after swelling changes and normal function resumes. Trigger: once return date is known and records have been transmitted. Risk: preventable instability, emergency visits, and prosthetic damage.
→ Use trend-based symptom capture for the first 30 days, not memory-based reporting — Rationale: worsening over 24 hours is the operational threshold that separates routine recovery from escalation. Trigger: every day for the first 7 days, then every 3 days through day 30, and after any new symptom. Risk: late detection of bite interference, soft-tissue deterioration, or hardware instability.
→ If pain, swelling, looseness, bleeding, drainage, bad taste, or bite interference worsens over a 24-hour period, escalate the same day to the treating channel and log the contact — Rationale: deterioration after return can move from manageable to urgent quickly. Trigger: any worsening symptom trend. Risk: infection progression, provisional damage, and fragmented emergency care.
→ Treat the Cavallini lifetime warranty as a documentation-dependent system, not automatic protection — Rationale: claims require proof of treatment details, maintenance, timely notice, and evidence retention. Trigger: after every follow-up visit, symptom report, maintenance event, or clinic communication. Risk: denied coverage, uncovered repairs, and out-of-pocket return travel.
→ If any scheduled hygiene or surveillance review is missed by more than 30 days, open a maintenance deviation entry and reschedule immediately — Rationale: missed maintenance raises both clinical and warranty risk. Trigger: any overdue recurring appointment. Risk: tissue deterioration, prosthetic complications, and weakened warranty position.
Decision Guide
U.S. provider confirmed before final return
If: A named U.S. office has agreed in writing or documented conversation to provide surveillance, emergency triage, routine maintenance, or another defined role, and a first review window is identified.
Then: Record the office contact, accepted scope, and first review timing in the provider inventory and proceed to packet transfer.
Else: Keep the case in active provider search, continue contacting offices from the provider inventory, and do not classify the Costa Rica phase as operationally closed.
Escalate when: No U.S. provider has affirmatively accepted any role before the final return home.
Handoff packet complete enough for U.S. triage
If: The packet includes procedure summary, treatment dates, provider names, implant brand if applicable, prosthetic details if provided, medications, post-op instructions, and supplied images or radiographs.
Then: Duplicate the packet to cloud and local storage, transmit it within 48 hours of arrival home, and request readability confirmation.
Else: Open a records recovery action with Cavallini the same business day and flag the missing items in the handoff checklist and warranty register.
Escalate when: Implant-related treatment lacks implant brand identification or procedure dates for each implant-related phase.
First U.S. review timing
If: The case involved implants, implant-supported bridges, or All-on-X and no different written review timing was provided by Cavallini.
Then: Schedule the first U.S. review for 7 to 14 days after return and use that date as the anchor for early surveillance and documentation.
Else: For crown-only or adjustment-only cases, schedule review based on symptom presence and complexity, but do not leave follow-up open-ended.
Escalate when: No review date is scheduled after return for a surgical or multi-implant case.
Symptom trend requires same-day escalation
If: Pain, swelling, looseness, bleeding, bite interference, foul taste, drainage, or tissue irritation is worsening over a 24-hour period instead of improving or stabilizing.
Then: Contact the treating channel specified in discharge instructions the same day, notify the U.S. provider or alternate local provider, and document symptoms, photos, and contact time in the issue capture form and warranty register.
Else: Continue routine surveillance according to the 30-day schedule and compare each entry against the prior entry for trend classification.
Escalate when: Swelling increases after initial decline, foul taste or drainage appears, or bite prevents normal soft-food chewing for more than 48 hours after return.
U.S. office received readable records
If: The office confirms the files were received, can be opened, and are sufficient to review the case.
Then: Log the acknowledgement date and proceed with scheduling or maintaining the planned first review.
Else: Resend files, follow up by phone within 2 business days, and request missing or reformatted files from Cavallini immediately.
Escalate when: No acknowledgement is received within 2 business days of packet transmission.
Provider role sufficient for case complexity
If: The chosen U.S. provider will handle the level of support the case requires, such as maintenance, occlusal checks, emergency triage, or implant-related assessment.
Then: Classify the provider as active coverage and record the exact limits of authority in the provider inventory.
Else: Classify the office as partial coverage only and continue building at least one backup pathway from the provider inventory.
Escalate when: An All-on-X or multi-implant case has only a provider willing to take emergency X-rays or offer minimal triage.
Maintenance schedule adequate for warranty preservation
If: Cleaning, surveillance imaging, restorative checks, and any written Cavallini maintenance expectations are assigned to actual dates or date ranges.
Then: Build the recurring calendar, store completion evidence, and route every finished event into the warranty register.
Else: Mark unknown intervals as REQUIRES CLARIFICATION and obtain written guidance rather than assuming the warranty remains valid without maintenance.
Escalate when: A hygiene or surveillance review is missed by more than 30 days.
Trigger Blocks
  • Trigger: 72 hours before departure from Costa Rica with no named U.S. follow-up office confirmed. Immediate response: activate ws-07-a, contact providers from the inventory, document accepted scope, and keep the case in active stabilization status.
  • Trigger: Final checkout at Cavallini or departure from the onsite hotel area. Immediate response: run ws-07-b, verify packet completeness, and duplicate all files to cloud and local access storage before leaving.
  • Trigger: Arrival home plus 48 hours. Immediate response: transmit the packet to the U.S. office, request written readability confirmation, and log the transmission in ws-07-d.
  • Trigger: Implant, implant-supported bridge, or All-on-X case returned to the U.S. Immediate response: schedule the first U.S. review within 7 to 14 days unless Cavallini gave a different written interval.
  • Trigger: Any symptom trend worsens over a 24-hour period. Immediate response: complete a same-day entry in ws-07-c, notify the instructed treatment channel, and preserve photo evidence.
  • Trigger: Bite prevents normal soft-food chewing for more than 48 hours after return. Immediate response: request earliest possible occlusal review with the U.S. provider or Cavallini-directed local provider.
  • Trigger: U.S. office says imaging files cannot be opened or documentation is insufficient. Immediate response: resend files, request corrected or missing records from Cavallini the same business day, and keep the handoff task open.
  • Trigger: Any maintenance visit, imaging event, symptom report, or clinic communication occurs. Immediate response: create or update the evidence entry in ws-07-d and store supporting files.
Risk Blocks
  • Risk: Returning home without a committed U.S. provider. Mitigation: confirm one named office before departure, record accepted scope, and maintain a backup provider path for complex implant cases.
  • Risk: Implant brand, component, or procedure-date data missing from the handoff packet. Mitigation: classify packet as incomplete, request records immediately from Cavallini, and do not close the handoff task until recovered.
  • Risk: Early bite interference or provisional instability goes undocumented. Mitigation: use ws-07-c on the required schedule and route worsening trends the same day.
  • Risk: U.S. provider refuses intervention because records are unreadable or incomplete. Mitigation: obtain readability acknowledgement within 2 business days and maintain duplicate packet storage in accessible formats.
  • Risk: Warranty claim weakened by undocumented phone calls or missing evidence. Mitigation: log every material communication, symptom event, photo set, and recommendation in ws-07-d with dates and storage location.
  • Risk: Missed maintenance creates biological, prosthetic, and warranty exposure. Mitigation: maintain ws-07-e as a controlled calendar and open a maintenance deviation entry for any visit missed by more than 30 days.
  • Risk: Patient assumes normal recovery when swelling, drainage, or bad taste indicate deterioration. Mitigation: use trend-based symptom review and escalate the same day when symptoms worsen over 24 hours.
  • Risk: Single-provider dependency for a complex All-on-X or multi-implant case. Mitigation: classify limited-scope offices as partial coverage only and maintain at least one backup specialist pathway.
Worksheet Linkage
U.S. Dentist Follow-Up Provider Inventory for Cavallini Restorative Cases
Use when: Use within 72 hours before departure from Costa Rica and again after return if the first office declines, delays, or offers only partial support.
Unblocks: It enables confirmed stateside surveillance, emergency triage routing, and backup provider coverage for implant or full-arch complications.
Post-Return Dental Handoff Packet Completeness Checklist for U.S. Providers
Use when: Use at final checkout in Costa Rica and before transmitting records to the U.S. provider.
Unblocks: It enables readable transfer of implant and prosthetic records and reduces refusal or delay by the U.S. office.
First-30-Days Bite, Provisional, and Soft-Tissue Issue Capture Form After Return Home
Use when: Use daily for the first 7 days after return, then every 3 days through day 30, and immediately after any new symptom appears.
Unblocks: It enables trend-based escalation, targeted provider communication, and documented early complication management.
Cavallini Lifetime Warranty Compliance and Claim-Evidence Register
Use when: Use starting at final checkout and update after every provider visit, symptom event, maintenance action, and communication with Cavallini or U.S. offices.
Unblocks: It enables warranty preservation, claim support, dispute resolution, and reconstruction of the care timeline.
Long-Term Cleaning, Imaging, and Prosthetic Review Schedule for Costa Rica Restorative Work
Use when: Use after the first post-return stabilization phase and whenever Cavallini or the U.S. provider defines maintenance intervals.
Unblocks: It enables routine maintenance control, overdue-visit escalation, and long-term treatment stability tracking.
Detailed Explanation

This chapter exists because major restorative treatment is not operationally finished when you leave Costa Rica. It has only changed custody. The clinical work may be complete for that phase, but continuity, warranty protection, and early complication control now depend on how well the case transfers into the U.S. care environment. That is why the chapter starts before the return flight rather than after you get home.

The first control objective is provider confirmation. A usable U.S. provider relationship is not a vague promise that an office might see you if needed. It is a documented agreement from one named office to perform surveillance, emergency triage, routine maintenance, or another clearly bounded role. This distinction matters because many offices will accept limited involvement with foreign restorative work, especially complex implant or full-arch cases. If the office will only take emergency X-rays, that is partial coverage, not full continuity.

The second control objective is packet integrity. The handoff packet is the translation layer between Cavallini's treatment environment and your U.S. provider's intervention environment. For crown-only work, a lighter packet may still be enough if it includes treatment dates, materials or prosthetic details when available, medications, instructions, and post-op notes. For implants, implant-supported bridges, and All-on-X, the packet must support component-level understanding. If implant identity data, timing, and prosthetic status are missing, the U.S. provider may delay even simple intervention because they cannot safely assume compatibility or reconstruct what was done abroad.

The third control objective is early trend capture. Many destabilizing issues appear only after swelling changes, diet expands, and medication support decreases. A bite that seemed acceptable on day 1 may be clearly dysfunctional on day 5. A provisional may begin to rock only after repeated function. Soft-tissue irritation can intensify gradually until eating and hygiene both deteriorate. The issue capture form therefore creates a trend file, not a diary. Its purpose is to classify each change as improving, unchanged, or worsening so routing decisions become operational rather than emotional.

The fourth control objective is same-day escalation discipline. This system does not require you to decide whether every symptom is an emergency in absolute clinical terms. It requires you to respond when the pattern worsens over a 24-hour period, when swelling increases after declining, when foul taste or drainage appears, or when bite interference blocks normal soft-food chewing beyond 48 hours. Those are operational escalation thresholds. Once crossed, the case moves from routine monitoring into same-day contact, documentation

…

Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 55
Chapter 7 · Quick Reference
domain-07

Stateside Dentist Handoff, 30-Day Complication Capture, Cavallini Warranty Compliance, and Maintenance Scheduling

Operating Rules
01 Do not treat the Costa Rica phase as complete until a U.S. provider has affirmatively accepted a role in your follow-up — Rationale: post-return instability often appears after travel, not before it. Trigger: within 72 hours before departure and again before final return home. Risk: unmanaged complications, treatment delay, and refusal of local evaluation.
02 Do not leave Costa Rica without a duplicated handoff packet containing implant system details, prosthetic records, medications, instructions, dates, and provider names — Rationale: U.S. offices cannot safely triage undocumented foreign restorative work. Trigger: final checkout after surgery, provisional delivery, or final prosthetic delivery. Risk: cross-border records chase, delayed intervention, and warranty ambiguity.
03 Treat unreadable files or missing implant data as a control failure, not a minor admin problem — Rationale: platform and component details affect local intervention scope. Trigger: when the U.S. office reports imaging cannot be opened or records are incomplete. Risk: duplicate diagnostics, delayed mechanical care, and inability to verify compatibility.
04 For implant, implant-supported bridge, or All-on-X cases, anchor the first U.S. review to a scheduled date within 7 to 14 days after return unless Cavallini directs otherwise — Rationale: bite problems, loosening, and soft-tissue issues often emerge after swelling changes and normal function resumes. Trigger: once return date is known and records have been transmitted. Risk: preventable instability, emergency visits, and prosthetic damage.
05 Use trend-based symptom capture for the first 30 days, not memory-based reporting — Rationale: worsening over 24 hours is the operational threshold that separates routine recovery from escalation. Trigger: every day for the first 7 days, then every 3 days through day 30, and after any new symptom. Risk: late detection of bite interference, soft-tissue deterioration, or hardware instability.
06 If pain, swelling, looseness, bleeding, drainage, bad taste, or bite interference worsens over a 24-hour period, escalate the same day to the treating channel and log the contact — Rationale: deterioration after return can move from manageable to urgent quickly. Trigger: any worsening symptom trend. Risk: infection progression, provisional damage, and fragmented emergency care.
07 Treat the Cavallini lifetime warranty as a documentation-dependent system, not automatic protection — Rationale: claims require proof of treatment details, maintenance, timely notice, and evidence retention. Trigger: after every follow-up visit, symptom report, maintenance event, or clinic communication. Risk: denied coverage, uncovered repairs, and out-of-pocket return travel.
08 If any scheduled hygiene or surveillance review is missed by more than 30 days, open a maintenance deviation entry and reschedule immediately — Rationale: missed maintenance raises both clinical and warranty risk. Trigger: any overdue recurring appointment. Risk: tissue deterioration, prosthetic complications, and weakened warranty position.
Worksheets
→ U.S. Dentist Follow-Up Provider Inventory for Cavallini Restorative Cases
→ Post-Return Dental Handoff Packet Completeness Checklist for U.S. Providers
→ First-30-Days Bite, Provisional, and Soft-Tissue Issue Capture Form After Return Home
→ Cavallini Lifetime Warranty Compliance and Claim-Evidence Register
→ Long-Term Cleaning, Imaging, and Prosthetic Review Schedule for Costa Rica Restorative Work
Cascade Triggers

Downstream impacts if this chapter fails.

! When surgery or prosthetic delivery details are received from Onsite Treatment Execution, Sedation Day Control, and Clinical Handoff Management, trigger handoff packet assembly and provider-transfer preparation in U.S. Follow-Up Continuity, Warranty Preservation, and Long-Term Maintenance Control.
! When the return packet is complete and travel home is cleared in Costa Rica Recovery Monitoring and Return-to-Travel Clearance, trigger U.S. provider transmission, readability confirmation, and first-review scheduling in U.S. Follow-Up Continuity, Warranty Preservation, and Long-Term Maintenance Control.
! When the U.S. follow-up provider receives the full treatment packet, trigger the first post-return review and complication surveillance cycle in U.S. Follow-Up Continuity, Warranty Preservation, and Long-Term Maintenance Control.
! When a worsening symptom trend is logged during the first 30 days after return, trigger same-day escalation to the discharge-instruction treatment channel and local provider triage in U.S. Follow-Up Continuity, Warranty Preservation, and Long-Term Maintenance Control.
! When warranty-required maintenance or documentation deadlines are identified, trigger recurring maintenance scheduling and evidence-retention actions in U.S. Follow-Up Continuity, Warranty Preservation, and Long-Term Maintenance Control.
! When all 30-day issues have dispositions and the first review is complete, trigger transition from active stabilization to recurring maintenance control within U.S. Follow-Up Continuity, Warranty Preservation, and Long-Term Maintenance Control.
Key Contacts
Role Name / Contact
Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 56
CAVALLINI COSTA RICA RESTORATIVE DENTAL TRAVEL CONTROL SYSTEM Chapter 7 — Stateside Dentist Handoff, 30-Day Complication Capture…
Worksheet TABLE → domain-07

U.S. Dentist Follow-Up Provider Inventory for Cavallini Restorative Cases

Produces a ranked inventory of U.S. dental offices willing to handle surveillance, urgent triage, or maintenance after Cavallini treatment in Costa Rica.

⏱ 40 minutes on first build, 10 minutes per provider update ↗ See also: Post-Return Dental Handoff Packet Completeness Checklist for U.S. Providers · First-30-Days Bite, Provisional, and Soft-Tissue Issue Capture Form After Return Home
Example entry
# U.S. Dental Office and City/State for Post-Costa-Rica Follow-Up Named Staff Member Who Confirmed Foreign-Case Acceptance Accepted Scope for Cavallini Aftercare (Emergency Triage / 7-14 Day Review / Hygiene Maintenance / Prosthetic Adjustment) Will Office Evaluate Nobel Biocare or Zimmer Implant Cases Placed Abroad? Required Records Before Scheduling (Implant Brand / Procedure Summary / Radiographs / Medication List) Earliest Available Post-Return Appointment Window Inventory Status (Primary / Backup / Partial Coverage / Declined)
1 North Shore Prosthodontics — Evanston, IL Melissa R., Treatment Coordinator — confirmed by phone 03/12/2026 7-14 Day Review + emergency triage + hygiene maintenance; prosthetic adjustment only after doctor review Yes — Nobel Biocare and Zimmer accepted if implant records provided Implant brand, procedure summary, discharge meds, panoramic image or CBCT, provisional/final prosthesis status Within 9 days of return if packet received 48 hours in advance Primary
2 Downtown Oral Surgery Group — Tampa, FL Carlos M., Surgical Scheduler — email reply 03/14/2026 Emergency triage and imaging only; no routine hygiene or prosthetic maintenance Yes — will evaluate both, but component treatment depends on records Procedure date, implant brand, list of symptoms, any post-op radiographs from Cavallini Same-week urgent slot if swelling, drainage, or mobility reported Partial Coverage
# U.S. Dental Office and City/State for Post-Costa-Rica Follow-Up Named Staff Member Who Confirmed Foreign-Case Acceptance Accepted Scope for Cavallini Aftercare (Emergency Triage / 7-14 Day Review / Hygiene Maintenance / Prosthetic Adjustment) Will Office Evaluate Nobel Biocare or Zimmer Implant Cases Placed Abroad? Required Records Before Scheduling (Implant Brand / Procedure Summary / Radiographs / Medication List) Earliest Available Post-Return Appointment Window Inventory Status (Primary / Backup / Partial Coverage / Declined)
1
2

4
