12
! Common Errors to Avoid
  • Recording only the main scheduling line and not the specific staff member who confirmed willingness to see a recent Cavallini patient with implants or All-on-X work
  • Marking a provider as full follow-up coverage when the office only agreed to emergency X-rays or visual triage for foreign restorative work
  • Failing to record whether the office will work with Nobel Biocare or Zimmer implant cases, forcing delays when component-specific issues arise
Decision Gates
Decision Gate — g-07-a-01 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Named U.S. Follow-Up Provider Confirmed Before Final Return
At least one U.S. office is marked Primary and includes a named contact, accepted scope of care, required records list, and an appointment window after return.
Pass → Proceed
Use the Primary office for packet transmission and first post-return review scheduling.
Fail → Remediate
Keep the case in active provider search, continue calling offices from this inventory, and do not treat the Costa Rica phase as operationally closed.
Decision Gate — g-07-a-02 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Case Complexity Matches Provider Support Level
For implant-supported bridge, multi-implant, or All-on-X cases, the chosen provider accepts more than emergency X-rays only and can perform at least surveillance or coordinated triage.
Pass → Proceed
Classify the office as sufficient continuity coverage and proceed to handoff packet transmission.
Fail → Remediate
Downgrade the office to Partial Coverage and continue building backup specialist pathways.
Date Completed
Reviewed By
Repeat-use worksheet · Photocopy as needed
Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 57
CAVALLINI COSTA RICA RESTORATIVE DENTAL TRAVEL CONTROL SYSTEM Chapter 7 — Stateside Dentist Handoff, 30-Day Complication Capture…
Worksheet CHECKLIST → domain-07

Post-Return Dental Handoff Packet Completeness Checklist for U.S. Providers

Verifies that the Cavallini treatment packet is complete, duplicated, transmitted to the U.S. provider, and acknowledged as readable for local follow-up care.

⏱ 30 minutes at Costa Rica checkout, 15 minutes after U.S. transmission ↗ See also: U.S. Dentist Follow-Up Provider Inventory for Cavallini Restorative Cases · Cavallini Lifetime Warranty Compliance and Claim-Evidence Register
What a verified item looks like
✓
Verify that implant brand information for each implant-related phase is explicitly documented as Nobel Biocare, Zimmer, or another named system — source checked: Cavallini surgical summary dated 04/18/2026 and implant card PDF; pass = Nobel Biocare listed for implants #3, #5, #12, and #14; action taken = filed in cloud folder and sent to North Shore Prosthodontics on 04/21/2026.
Verify that the Cavallini procedure summary for the most recent treatment phase is in hand before leaving the clinic or onsite hotel area — source: printed discharge paperwork or patient coordinator packet; pass = procedure performed, treatment date, and treating provider names are listed; fail = request corrected discharge paperwork from Cavallini the same day and do not depart with the packet marked complete.
Verify that implant brand information for each implant-related phase is explicitly documented as Nobel Biocare, Zimmer, or another named system — source: surgical summary, implant card, or component sheet; pass = brand is named for every implant event; fail = open a records recovery request with Cavallini before airport departure.
Verify that each implant-related document shows the actual procedure date tied to that phase of care — source: operative note, discharge summary, or appointment receipt with clinical detail; pass = every implant placement or prosthetic delivery date is traceable; fail = request dated confirmation from Cavallini and classify the packet as incomplete.
Verify that the packet states whether the delivered prosthesis is provisional, immediate-load, temporary bridge, final crown, or final full-arch prosthesis — source: restorative delivery note or written treatment summary; pass = prosthesis status is explicitly named; fail = ask Cavallini to issue a clarifying note because U.S. providers cannot infer this safely.
Verify that post-operative medication details are documented with drug name, dosage, and timing — source: Cavallini prescription printout or discharge medication sheet; pass = every active medication is listed in readable form; fail = request a replacement medication list from the clinic or onsite pharmacy immediately.
Verify that Cavallini post-op instructions are included in a format the patient can read without internet access — source: printed discharge instructions or downloaded PDF saved locally; pass = a local offline copy exists; fail = save or print the instructions before leaving San José.
Verify that any images or radiographs supplied by Cavallini are copied into both a cloud folder and a local device folder — source: USB drive, email attachment, patient portal download, or messaging transfer; pass = two accessible copies exist in separate locations; fail = duplicate the files before travel day ends.
Verify that the file names for radiographs or imaging exports identify the treatment phase and date — source: digital imaging folder; pass = files can be matched to the treatment event without guessing; fail = rename and organize files before transmitting them to the U.S. provider.
Verify that provider names from Cavallini are captured for the dentist, surgeon, or prosthodontic lead involved in the recent phase — source: treatment summary, clinic correspondence, invoice with provider details; pass = at least one responsible treating clinician is named; fail = request provider attribution from the patient coordinator.
Verify that any warranty document, warranty enrollment condition, or written warranty summary from Cavallini is included in the packet — source: clinic warranty paperwork, quote terms, or emailed policy language; pass = warranty evidence is stored with the packet; fail = request the written warranty terms and note the gap in the warranty register.
Verify that the packet includes the return-home date and planned U.S. review timing — source: travel itinerary and provider scheduling record; pass = the U.S. office can see when the patient returned and when follow-up is expected; fail = add a cover sheet before transmission.
Verify that the packet includes the patient’s current symptom status at departure if any pain, swelling, bite issue, or soft-tissue irritation was present — source: recovery notes or day-of-departure symptom log; pass = the U.S. provider receives a baseline status; fail = create a dated baseline note immediately.
Verify that the U.S. provider’s secure email, upload link, or fax route for foreign dental records has been confirmed before sending files — source: direct call with the office; pass = a named route and recipient are documented; fail = do not send to a generic inbox without confirmation.
Verify that the full packet was transmitted within 48 hours of arriving home — source: sent email, portal upload confirmation, fax confirmation page, or courier tracking; pass = transmission timestamp is within the required window; fail = send the packet the same business day and log the delay.
Verify that the U.S. office acknowledged receipt of the packet in writing — source: email reply, portal message, or fax confirmation plus staff email; pass = a dated acknowledgement exists; fail = follow up by phone and resend the packet within 2 business days.
Verify that the U.S. office confirmed the attachments are readable, especially imaging files — source: written reply from the office after opening the files; pass = office states files opened successfully; fail = request missing or alternate-format files from Cavallini the same business day.
Verify that any unreadable CBCT, panoramic, or radiograph files have an active recovery request logged with Cavallini — source: sent email or message thread to the clinic; pass = request date, contact route, and missing file description are documented; fail = open the request immediately and copy the U.S. office if needed.
Verify that the patient has one cloud-stored packet folder and one local offline copy accessible during travel disruptions or urgent visits — source: phone, laptop, tablet, or printed binder; pass = both copies are available; fail = duplicate before relying on the packet for emergency care.
Verify that the packet includes a concise cover note for the U.S. dentist describing the Costa Rica procedure scope and requested follow-up role — source: patient-prepared summary using this chapter; pass = the office can see exactly whether surveillance, triage, or maintenance is requested; fail = create the cover note before the first appointment.
Verify that any missing packet element has been marked as an open recovery item rather than silently omitted — source: packet index or missing-items log; pass = each missing item has a recovery action and date; fail = classify the handoff as incomplete and continue records retrieval.
Verify that the first post-return review is scheduled or explicitly declined by the U.S. office after packet review — source: appointment confirmation, call log, or office email; pass = appointment date or documented decline exists; fail = move to the next office in the provider inventory.
Verify that the final packet folder includes communication proof for every resend, correction, or file replacement related to the handoff — source: email thread, portal screenshots, or fax logs; pass = transmission history is reconstructable; fail = save all evidence to the warranty register folder immediately.
! Common Errors to Avoid
  • Leaving Costa Rica with payment receipts and selfies but without the implant brand, procedure dates, or prosthesis status that a U.S. provider needs to intervene safely
  • Assuming emailed files were usable without obtaining written confirmation that the U.S. office could actually open the radiographs or attachment formats
  • Storing the only copy of the handoff packet in cloud storage that cannot be accessed during travel or if internet service fails
Decision Gates
Decision Gate — g-07-b-01 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Implant and Prosthetic Packet Sufficiency for U.S. Triage
The packet identifies implant brand and procedure date for each implant-related phase, names prosthesis status, and includes readable supporting records.
Pass → Proceed
Transmit to the U.S. provider and proceed to scheduling the first review.
Fail → Remediate
Keep the return-to-U.S. handoff open, request missing records from Cavallini immediately, and log the deficiency in the warranty register.
Decision Gate — g-07-b-02 BLOCKS COMPLETION
U.S. Provider Readability Acknowledgement Received
The receiving U.S. office has confirmed in writing that the packet was received and files are readable within 2 business days.
Pass → Proceed
Treat the provider handoff as operationally transferred and anchor the first review date.
Fail → Remediate
Follow up by phone, resend the packet, and escalate to the next office in the provider inventory if unresolved.
Date Completed
Reviewed By
Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 58
CAVALLINI COSTA RICA RESTORATIVE DENTAL TRAVEL CONTROL SYSTEM Chapter 7 — Stateside Dentist Handoff, 30-Day Complication Capture…
Worksheet → domain-07

First-30-Days Bite, Provisional, and Soft-Tissue Issue Capture Form After Return Home

Creates a structured symptom and escalation log for the first 30 days after returning from Cavallini so changes in function, comfort, or tissue health are routed quickly and documented for care and warranty support.

⏱ 8 minutes per entry for days 1-7, then 8 minutes every 3 days or when new symptoms appear ↗ See also: U.S. Dentist Follow-Up Provider Inventory for Cavallini Restorative Cases · Cavallini Lifetime Warranty Compliance and Claim-Evidence Register
Entry Timing and Return-Home Surveillance Cycle Control
s-07-c-01
Before you start

Before completing this section, retrieve your return-home date, Cavallini discharge instructions, and any instruction about when the first U.S. follow-up review should occur. Also review the chapter rule that entries are required daily for the first 7 days after return, then every 3 days through day 30, plus immediately after any new symptom appears. Complete this section at the top of every issue-capture entry so the symptom timeline can be reconstructed later. This section is complete when the entry date, day-from-return count, treatment phase being monitored, and next required check date are all explicitly recorded.

Date this post-return symptom check is being recorded * date
__ / __ / ________
Enter the actual date of the observation, such as 2026-04-24 for day 3 after landing back in the U.S.
Day count since landing back in the United States from San José * number
3
Use a whole number tied to the return flight date; e.g., 3 means third day after return home.
Cavallini treatment phase currently being monitored * select
Implant-supported provisional bridge delivered in Costa Rica
Select: Single crown or bridge treatment without surgery / Implant placement only / Implant-supported provisional bridge delivery / All-on-X immediate-load provisional / Final prosthesis delivery / Adjustment-only post-op phase / REQUIRES CLARIFICATION
Choose the exact phase being observed, such as All-on-X provisional delivery or single crown placement.
Was this entry triggered by the scheduled surveillance cycle or by a new symptom event? * select
Scheduled day-3 surveillance entry
Select: Scheduled surveillance entry / New symptom event after normal recovery / Provider-requested recheck / Post-appointment follow-up entry
Choose whether the log was routine or prompted by a new concern, such as New symptom event after chewing dinner.
Date of most recent prior issue-capture entry for comparison date
__ / __ / ________
Enter the previous entry date when available so trend classification is possible.
Next required symptom-check date based on this chapter's 30-day monitoring cadence * date
__ / __ / ________
For days 1-7 enter the next day; later entries may be 3 days later unless a provider instructed otherwise.
Bite Function, Prosthesis Stability, and Eating-Tolerance Observation
s-07-c-02
Before you start

Before completing this section, review your previous issue-capture entry and, if relevant, think about your most recent chewing attempt, speech difficulty, or sensation of movement in the provisional or prosthesis. Use actual function testing from soft-food chewing and normal speaking rather than a vague overall impression. This section is complete when bite contact pattern, chewing ability, mobility concerns, and speech change status are recorded with enough detail that either Cavallini or a U.S. dentist could triage next steps without guessing. If the treatment was non-surgical crown work only, still complete the bite and chewing fields because occlusal interference may emerge after swelling changes.

Where does the bite hit first or feel overloaded when teeth come together after Cavallini treatment? *
Heavy first contact on upper right provisional bridge near back chewing side; left side feels delayed to touch
Describe the exact contact pattern, such as heavy on right posterior provisional and cannot get even bilateral contact.
Can you chew soft foods on both sides without pain, rocking, or forced one-sided chewing? * select
Soft foods possible on left only
Select: Yes, both sides / Yes, but only one side comfortably / Only very soft foods / No, chewing is not functional / Not tested yet today
Choose the option that matches actual function, not hoped-for function.
Any sensation that the provisional bridge, crown, screw-retained unit, or full-arch prosthesis is moving or rocking * boolean
Mark true if there is any perceptible loosening, clicking, or rocking during chewing or tongue pressure.
If movement or clicking is present, describe exactly when it happens and which restoration feels unstable
Small click on right side of upper full-arch provisional when biting scrambled eggs; no visible break but feels less stable than yesterday
Record the location, trigger, and comparison to prior entries, such as rocking only during chewing on left molar area.
Speech change noticed after return home (lisping, air escape, clicking, or altered pronunciation) * select
Mild lisp worse than day 1, mostly on S sounds
Select: No speech change today / Mild but improving speech change / Unchanged speech difficulty / Worsening speech change / Speech change not applicable to this treatment type
Choose the most accurate speech status since return.
Compared with the prior entry, the bite and chewing function are * select
Worsening
Select: Improving / Unchanged / Worsening / No prior entry available
Trend classification drives routing; e.g., Worsening if chewing is harder than at the last entry.
Pain, Swelling, Bleeding, Taste, and Soft-Tissue Trend Capture
s-07-c-03
Before you start

Before completing this section, gather your prior symptom entry, current medication list, and any photographs taken of the gums, incision areas, or prosthesis contact points. Observe trend, not just intensity: note whether swelling has declined, stayed the same, or increased over the last 24 hours. This section is complete when pain level, swelling pattern, bleeding or drainage, odor or bad taste, and tissue irritation are documented clearly enough to decide whether routine monitoring or same-day escalation is required. If anything is worsening instead of stabilizing, be ready to route immediately using the escalation section that follows.

Current pain level related to the Cavallini-treated area on a 0-10 scale * number
5
Use a whole number from 0 to 10, such as 5 for moderate pain requiring attention but not emergency-level distress.
Pain trend versus the last issue-capture entry * select
Worsening over last 24 hours
Select: Improving / Unchanged / Worsening over last 24 hours / No prior entry available
Choose the trend, such as Improving after medication taper or Worsening over last 24 hours.
Swelling pattern at the implant, gum, cheek, or jaw area since the prior entry * select
Increased after initial decline
Select: No swelling present / Expected mild swelling and improving / Unchanged swelling / Increased after initial decline / New swelling appeared today
An increase after an initial decline is a same-day escalation trigger in this chapter.
Any bleeding, drainage, foul taste, or persistent bad odor coming from the treated area * select
Foul taste with light drainage near upper left implant site
Select: Light bleeding only / Persistent bleeding / Foul taste or odor / Drainage/pus-like fluid / Multiple of the above
Choose the closest match and use the next field for detail if present.
Exact soft-tissue problem observed today around the Costa Rica restorative work *
Red sore spot under right side of temporary bridge rubbing cheek; eating causes sharp irritation and sleep was interrupted last night
State location, trigger, and function impact, such as ulcer under provisional flange causing eating difficulty.
Medication use today for this issue, including whether relief was adequate *
Ibuprofen 600 mg at 8 AM and 2 PM; relief lasted about 3 hours and pain returned with chewing
List actual drug, dose, timing, and response rather than simply writing taking meds.
Same-Day Escalation Routing and Evidence Preservation
s-07-c-04
Before you start

Before completing this section, review the decision thresholds in this chapter: worsening pain, swelling, looseness, bleeding, bite interference, foul taste, or drainage over a 24-hour period require same-day contact. Gather any photographs, temperature readings if relevant, and the direct contact routes for both Cavallini and the selected U.S. provider from the provider inventory. This section is complete when the entry states whether escalation was required, who was contacted, when they were contacted, and where supporting evidence is stored. If no contact was made despite a worsening trigger, record the reason and correct the failure the same day.

Does today's symptom profile meet this chapter's same-day escalation threshold? * boolean
Mark true if symptoms are worsening, function is impaired, mobility exists, or foul taste/drainage is present.
Primary routing channel used for today's concern * select
Contacted both Cavallini and U.S. provider same day
Select: Routine monitoring only / Contacted U.S. provider only / Contacted Cavallini only / Contacted both Cavallini and U.S. provider same day / Attempted contact but no response yet
Choose the actual route taken based on urgency and provider availability.
Time and method of most important provider contact made for this entry
04/24/2026 3:40 PM Eastern — emailed symptom summary with photos to Melissa at North Shore Prosthodontics and WhatsApp message sent to Cavallini coordinator
Include date, time zone if useful, contact route, and recipient name.
What response or appointment instruction was received from Cavallini or the U.S. office
U.S. office moved review to 04/25/2026 10:30 AM; Cavallini asked for close-up photos and advised soft diet until seen
Record the actual disposition, not just called office.
Where today's photos, messages, and supporting evidence are stored for warranty and follow-up use *
Cloud folder: Cavallini Return Home > Day 6 Issue Photos; local iPhone album duplicated to laptop
Name the exact folder or storage path so evidence can be retrieved later.
Open next-step deadline created by today's issue entry *
Attend occlusal adjustment visit by 04/25/2026 and resend photos to Cavallini if mobility increases before appointment
State a dated next step, such as book urgent review by tomorrow morning or await same-day callback by 5 PM.
! Common Errors to Avoid
  • Writing vague comments like 'feels off' instead of recording which side hits first, whether chewing soft food is possible, and whether the symptom is improving or worsening versus the prior entry
  • Failing to compare the current entry to the previous entry, which hides the difference between expected healing and same-day escalation triggers
  • Not logging the exact date and contact route when Cavallini or the U.S. provider was notified about worsening pain, swelling, loosening, or drainage
Decision Gates
Decision Gate — g-07-c-01 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Worsening 24-Hour Trend Requires Same-Day Escalation
Pain, swelling, looseness, bleeding, foul taste/drainage, or bite interference has worsened over a 24-hour period rather than stabilizing or improving.
Pass → Proceed
Contact the treating channel specified in the discharge instructions the same day and log the contact details in this form.
Fail → Remediate
Do not leave the issue in routine monitoring; route simultaneously to Cavallini and a local provider from the inventory if the primary office is unavailable.
Decision Gate — g-07-c-02 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Persistent Bite Interference Beyond 48 Hours
The bite prevents even bilateral contact, causes repeated one-sided clicking, or makes normal soft-food chewing impossible for more than 48 hours after return.
Pass → Proceed
Schedule the earliest available occlusal review with the U.S. provider or Cavallini-directed local provider.
Fail → Remediate
Mark the case as active stabilization and continue urgent provider routing until an evaluation is secured.
Date Completed
Reviewed By
Repeat-use worksheet · Photocopy as needed
Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 59
CAVALLINI COSTA RICA RESTORATIVE DENTAL TRAVEL CONTROL SYSTEM Chapter 7 — Stateside Dentist Handoff, 30-Day Complication Capture…
Worksheet TABLE → domain-07

Cavallini Lifetime Warranty Compliance and Claim-Evidence Register

Maintains the evidentiary record of treatment dates, maintenance compliance, symptom notifications, provider communications, and stored proof needed to preserve Cavallini warranty rights.

⏱ 25 minutes to set up, 5 minutes per event logged ↗ See also: Post-Return Dental Handoff Packet Completeness Checklist for U.S. Providers · First-30-Days Bite, Provisional, and Soft-Tissue Issue Capture Form After Return Home · Long-Term Cleaning, Imaging, and Prosthetic Review Schedule for Costa Rica Restorative Work
Example entry
# Event Date Related to Cavallini Restorative Work Event Type (Treatment / Return Home / U.S. Review / Hygiene / Imaging / Reported Issue / Cavallini Contact / Missed Maintenance) Exact Issue or Maintenance Action Logged Evidence Stored (photo folder / visit summary / email thread / radiograph file) Who Was Notified or Involved (Cavallini / U.S. office / both) Response, Recommendation, or Claim-Relevant Outcome Warranty Compliance Status After This Entry
1 2026-04-18 Treatment Upper implant-supported provisional delivered at Cavallini; discharge packet received PDF discharge summary, panoramic image, prosthesis photos in cloud folder /Trip2_Discharge Cavallini Baseline treatment event established for later claim reference Compliant
2 2026-04-24 Reported Issue Day-6 bite overload on right side with clicking and reduced soft-food chewing Issue photos in /ReturnHome/Day6 and symptom log ws-07-c entry Both U.S. office moved review earlier; Cavallini requested photos and advised soft diet Compliant — documented and reported same day
# Event Date Related to Cavallini Restorative Work Event Type (Treatment / Return Home / U.S. Review / Hygiene / Imaging / Reported Issue / Cavallini Contact / Missed Maintenance) Exact Issue or Maintenance Action Logged Evidence Stored (photo folder / visit summary / email thread / radiograph file) Who Was Notified or Involved (Cavallini / U.S. office / both) Response, Recommendation, or Claim-Relevant Outcome Warranty Compliance Status After This Entry

! Common Errors to Avoid
  • Assuming a phone call to Cavallini preserves warranty rights without saving the date, summary, and any follow-up message or email
  • Logging problems without linking them to photo locations, visit summaries, or maintenance records, leaving the claim file impossible to prove later
  • Failing to record missed hygiene or review visits as deviations, which weakens the ability to show compliance with lifetime warranty conditions
Decision Gates
Decision Gate — g-07-d-01 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Material Complication Must Be Documented to Preserve Warranty File
Any complication affecting function, appearance, comfort, hardware stability, or tissue health has a date, symptom description, provider notification record, and evidence location logged in the register.
Pass → Proceed
Continue claim-ready documentation and link supporting photos, visits, and communications to the entry.
Fail → Remediate
Treat the warranty file as noncompliant until retrospective documentation is gathered immediately.
Decision Gate — g-07-d-02
Missed Maintenance Over 30 Days Creates Deviation
A scheduled hygiene or surveillance review has been missed by more than 30 days.
Pass → Proceed
Open a maintenance deviation entry, reschedule immediately, and preserve proof of corrective action.
Fail → Remediate
Do not assume passive compliance; note that warranty leverage may be reduced until the deviation is corrected.
Date Completed
Reviewed By
Repeat-use worksheet · Photocopy as needed
Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 60
CAVALLINI COSTA RICA RESTORATIVE DENTAL TRAVEL CONTROL SYSTEM Chapter 7 — Stateside Dentist Handoff, 30-Day Complication Capture…
Worksheet TWO COLUMN → domain-07

Long-Term Cleaning, Imaging, and Prosthetic Review Schedule for Costa Rica Restorative Work

Builds the ongoing maintenance calendar that ties actual Cavallini restorative work to due dates, responsible providers, and missed-visit escalation rules in the United States.

⏱ 35 minutes to build, 10 minutes after each completed review ↗ See also: U.S. Dentist Follow-Up Provider Inventory for Cavallini Restorative Cases · First-30-Days Bite, Provisional, and Soft-Tissue Issue Capture Form After Return Home · Cavallini Lifetime Warranty Compliance and Claim-Evidence Register
Scheduled Maintenance Requirement for Cavallini Restorative Work
Completed or Planned U.S. Follow-Up Execution Record
Restorative Scope-to-Maintenance Obligation Mapping
Before you start

Before completing this section, gather the Cavallini discharge packet, treatment summary, warranty terms, and any written maintenance intervals already provided by the clinic. Also review the Post-Return Dental Handoff Packet Completeness Checklist so you are not building a schedule from incomplete records. This section is complete when each type of restorative work delivered in Costa Rica has a corresponding maintenance obligation, or the missing interval is explicitly marked REQUIRES CLARIFICATION. Do not assume that a lifetime warranty replaces the need for actual scheduled maintenance events.

Left side — Cavallini restorative work type that must drive the maintenance calendar *
Right side — U.S. provider or office assigned to carry out this maintenance obligation *
Left side — Cavallini-written maintenance interval or warranty-required timing for this work type *
Right side — Date this maintenance pathway was confirmed with the U.S. provider *
__ / __ / ________
__ / __ / ________
Hygiene, Surveillance Imaging, and Prosthetic Review Calendar Build
Before you start

Before completing this section, gather the first U.S. review date, any Cavallini instructions about radiographs or prosthesis checks, and the provider inventory showing who agreed to maintenance. Use real dates when known; if the timing is not supplied in writing, mark the schedule element REQUIRES CLARIFICATION rather than inventing an interval. This section is complete when at least the next hygiene visit, next imaging review, and next restorative review each have a due date or explicit clarification flag. The right column must show whether each event is already booked, completed, or still awaiting scheduling.

Left side — Next hygiene/periodontal maintenance due date tied to the Cavallini restorative case *
Right side — Current booking status for that hygiene maintenance event *
Left side — Next surveillance imaging due date for implant or bone-level review *
Right side — Imaging provider or radiology source expected to complete that review *
Left side — Next prosthetic integrity or bite review due date for the Costa Rica restoration *
Right side — Present status of that prosthetic review event *
Deviation Control for Missed Maintenance and Open Stabilization Issues
Before you start

Before completing this section, review the warranty register and the first-30-days issue capture log to see whether any symptoms remain unresolved or any maintenance event is already overdue. This chapter states that a missed hygiene or surveillance review by more than 30 days must generate a maintenance deviation entry. This section is complete when you can show whether the case is in routine maintenance mode or still in active stabilization, and when any overdue event has a dated corrective action. If open complications remain at day 30, do not mark the case as routine maintenance only.

Left side — Are any hygiene, imaging, or restorative review events currently more than 30 days overdue? *
Right side — If overdue, what corrective action date and rescheduling step were entered in the warranty register *
Left side — Current case mode based on symptom status and maintenance readiness *
Right side — Lead coordinator responsible for keeping the Cavallini maintenance calendar current *
Future-Date Control and Evidence Linkage
Before you start

Before completing this section, gather your calendar system, reminder settings, and the storage path used for visit summaries, radiographs, and warranty evidence. The goal here is not merely to list due dates but to ensure every planned event can be proven completed later if Cavallini requests maintenance evidence. This section is complete when reminder timing is set, evidence storage is named, and each completed visit can flow into the warranty register without reconstruction from memory. If your current calendar tool cannot support repeat reminders, identify the replacement method now.

Left side — Reminder lead time before each Cavallini-related maintenance event becomes due *
Right side — Calendar or task system where these reminders are actually stored *
Left side — Exact storage location for completed hygiene notes, radiographs, and prosthetic review summaries *
Right side — Date this maintenance schedule was last reconciled against actual completed visits *
__ / __ / ________
__ / __ / ________
! Common Errors to Avoid
  • Using a generic six-month cleaning assumption without checking whether Cavallini gave a different interval for implants, full-arch prosthetics, or warranty compliance
  • Failing to distinguish between provisional-phase reviews and long-term final prosthesis surveillance, causing the wrong interval to be tracked
  • Leaving future imaging or prosthetic review timing blank because the U.S. office did not suggest dates, instead of marking REQUIRES CLARIFICATION and obtaining written direction
Decision Gates
Decision Gate — g-07-e-01 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Written Maintenance Timing Exists or Is Flagged for Clarification
Every hygiene, imaging, and prosthetic review interval is either supported by a written Cavallini or U.S. provider instruction or explicitly marked REQUIRES CLARIFICATION.
Pass → Proceed
Build the calendar with real dates and proceed to reminder setup.
Fail → Remediate
Do not guess intervals; obtain written clarification and keep the schedule open until resolved.
Decision Gate — g-07-e-02 BLOCKS COMPLETION
Transition from Active Stabilization to Routine Maintenance
The first 30 days after return are complete without unmanaged complication, all documented issues have a disposition, and recurring maintenance dates are entered.
Pass → Proceed
Move the case to routine maintenance mode and continue logging completed events in the warranty register.
Fail → Remediate
Keep the case in active stabilization mode and continue provider follow-up until open issues are resolved.
Date Completed
Reviewed By
Repeat-use worksheet · Photocopy as needed

Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 66
Contingency Protocol

Rapid Response Reference

Failure Modes & Response Actions
!
The patient compares only Cavallini procedure pricing to a US quote and omits flights, hotel recovery days, medication, missed work, and revision reserve; the apparent savings disappears after commitment, creating mid-treatment funding shortfall.
!
US records are incomplete or too old, but travel is booked anyway; after arrival, the clinic changes scope or delays treatment because anatomy, infection, bone condition, or periodontal status differs from assumptions.
!
The patient authorizes implant placement, tooth preparation, or All-on-X extractions before final in-person confirmation of scope and cost; once teeth are altered or extracted, reversal is no longer possible and negotiating leverage is gone.
!
The patient undergoes IV sedation without a reliable escort or recovery support plan; discharge, medication compliance, and immediate complication monitoring fail during the highest-risk post-op period.
!
A post-operative issue such as bite instability, prosthetic fracture, swelling escalation, suspected infection, or implant concern emerges after return to the US, but no stateside dentist is willing to evaluate foreign work, delaying intervention beyond the easiest correction window.
!
The patient leaves Costa Rica without operative notes, implant system details, prosthetic specifications, or imaging; later US providers cannot service the case efficiently and warranty claims lack required documentation.
!
The patient assumes a 'lifetime warranty' guarantees no-cost correction under all conditions, but exclusions for maintenance failures, non-compliance, outside modifications, or missed reporting windows void practical coverage after the point of failure.
!
Flights are scheduled too tightly after surgery, causing the patient to travel while unstable, miss a required Cavallini review, or lose the chance to correct an immediate fit/function problem before international departure.
!
The patient relies on a single preliminary quote without documenting what triggers price changes for grafting, extractions, temporaries, sedation, or prosthetic upgrades; final invoice exceeds approved funds after arrival.
!
A US maintenance plan is never activated, leading to missed hygiene, occlusal adjustments, or early implant/prosthetic monitoring, with failures surfacing months later when corrective action is more invasive and expensive.
Operating Constraints
⊘ Treatment planning accuracy is limited by the completeness and recency of US diagnostic records before in-person evaluation in Costa Rica.
⊘ Major restorative work such as implants or All-on-X may require staged treatment over multiple Costa Rica trips rather than a single visit.
⊘ Irreversible dental decisions may occur under travel pressure because the patient is already in-country and financially committed to flights, lodging, and time off work.
⊘ Costa Rica treatment requires valid US traveler identification documents and sufficient travel flexibility to remain in-country if recovery or treatment timing changes.
⊘ If IV sedation is used, same-day self-management capacity is reduced and a discharge support plan may be operationally necessary.
⊘ The patient's final all-in cost is affected by exchange rates, card processing, wire timing, refund policies, and unplanned changes in treatment scope.
⊘ US dental insurance may provide limited or no reimbursement for out-of-country treatment, so the system must operate assuming high out-of-pocket exposure unless verified otherwise.
⊘ Continuity of care after return is constrained by whether a US dentist is willing to monitor or service work initiated by a foreign clinic.
⊘ Warranty preservation depends on compliance with Cavallini's written terms, documentation retention, maintenance behavior, and timely reporting of defects or complications.
⊘ Clinical compatibility for future maintenance is constrained by the specific restorative components and implant systems used, including Nobel Biocare or Zimmer where applicable.
⊘ Use of the clinic's onsite hotel, lab, pharmacy, and integrated infrastructure may improve convenience but does not eliminate the need for independent verification of records, costs, consent terms, and discharge readiness.
⊘ The system must account for Costa Rica as the treatment destination, including international flight timing, local transport in San José, and recovery in a foreign country away from the patient's normal medical support network.
Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System 70
